SRCCHARThe input source of the data on the record identified with a code value found on RF522
STACHARStatus

Column NameColumn AliasData TypeDescription

PRI_AHCCCS_IDCHARThe identification number assigned to a recipient of services in the AHCCCS program
RATE_CDCHARIdentifies what basis the rate was paid to the health plan for a recipient; Rate codes reflect the risk adjustment groups for medical plans and adult/child distinction for dental plans. The rate code also identifies which clients have Medicare coverage for SMI behavioral health.
SRCCHARThe input source of the data on the record identified with a code value found on RF522
STACHARIndicates whether the record is in active (A), inactive (I) or pended (P) status.

BHS_CATCHARA code that indicates the level of behavioral health care services the member is eligible.
BHS_SITECHARThe BHS site.
CAP_INDCHARIndicates the capitation type.
CAT_INDCHARIndicates whether or not an eligibility is categorical. Federal Funding =Y

CLIENT_IDCHARThis field designates the client id under which the recipient is known for the additional benefit program
CSACHARThe county code of the service area.
CTRT_TYPCHARThe type of contract held by the health plan.
DAT_REC_ADDEDDATEDate record added to health plan
DR_PLANCHARPart D Drug Plan ID

The primary objective, apart from confirming matching data, was to identify special characters that would cause issues with data loads.
MDC-ENRL-TYP (per Baiyina: in HPMMIS it is MDCD_ENRL_TYP)CHARThe type of Medicare enrollment of the recipient.
NBR_OF_DAYSNUMBERNumber of Days
PD_MMTHNUMBERThe number of days enrolled / the number of days in the month. This is a calculated field.
PPC_INDCHARPrior Period Coverage Indicator. Contains either a Yes or No value.
PRI_AHCCCS_IDCHARThe identification number assigned to a recipient of services in the AHCCCS program
RATE_CDCHARIdentifies what basis the capitation was paid to the health plan for a recipient. Values are in generic table "caprt" (transaction rf401)
RCP_AGENUMBERThis is the age of the recipient at time of eligibility.
RCP_SEXCHARThis field contains the sex code for an individual. Values are "F" (female) and "M" male
RISK_AGENUMBERAge of the recipient based on rate code characteristics
RISK_FNDCHARThe funding source for this record
RISK_MDCCHARMedicare Coverage Indicator
RISK_PGMCHARThe eligibility program for the record
RISK_SEXCHARThis field contains the sex code for an individual. Values are "f" (female) and "m" male
RISK_SUBCHARThe Subprogram category based on rate code characteristics
RVN_INDCHARAn indicator of whether or not a recipient is living on a reservation. Used in race, tribe and reservation edits
STACHARIndicates whether the record is in active (A), inactive (I) or pended (P) status.
TYPECHARType of FYI record
TYPE_IDCHARType ID is the BHS, CRS, etc. plan ID number.
T_RP_MDC_CVG
Column NameColumn AliasData TypeDescription
AHCCCS_IDCHARThe identification number assigned to a recipient of services MQD in the AHCCCS program
BEG_DATDATEBegin date of Medicare part record that is associated with a recipient
CHG_RSNCHARProvides the customer eligibility change reason code for Medicare end
DAT_REC_ADDEDDATEDate the Medicare record was added to PMMIS
END_DATDATEEnd date of Medicare part record that is associated with a recipient
LAST_MOD_DATDATEThe date on which the Medicare beneficiary's information was added, changed, or terminated
MBICHARCMS removed Social Security Number (SSN)-based Health Insurance Claim Numbers (HICNs) from Medicare cards and are now using Medicare Beneficiary Identifiers (MBIs) for Medicare transactions like billing, eligibility status, and claim status.
MBI_SRCCHARSource of MBI data
MDC_CLM_IDCHARHealth Insurance Claim
(HIC) Number (aka Medicare
Claim ID Number)
MDC_CVGCHARA code that indicates the possible different combinations of the recipient's Medicare coverage.
PAYR_IDCHARIdentifies who is paying the Medicare premium
PRI_AHCCCS_IDCHARThe identification number assigned to a recipient of services in the AHCCCS program
SRCCHARThe input source of the data on the record identified with a code value found on RF522
TERM_RSNCHARThe code identifying the reason for the termination of Medicare Coverage for the recipient.
UPD_IDCHARper Patrick, Per Patrick:
UPD_​ID on the T_​RP_​MDC_​CVG table is still currently a field on this table. However, it appears to be null for all records.
T_RP_MED_CND
Column NameColumn AliasData TypeDescription
AHCCCS_IDCHARThe identification number assigned to a recipient of services MQD in the AHCCCS program
AS_OF_DATDATEThe date on which the recipient's medical condition began
BEG_DATDATEThis is a begin date, and it's not the begin date of the recipient's medical condition. (Effective begin date for the medical condition record)
DAT_REC_ADDEDDATEThe date the data for the recipient's medical condition was first added to the AHCCCS system. (The date the record was added to the database)
END_DATMedical condition End DateDATEMedical condition End Date - date the medical condition ended
EXP_DEL_DATDATEThe expected delivery date for an expectant mother recipient.
EXP_DSCHG_DATDATEExpected discharge date of hospital admission related to the medical condition (The expected discharge date of a recipient who is in the hospital)
HOSP_ADM_DATDATEDate of hospital admission related to the medical condition
LAST_MOD_DATDATEThe date the data for the recipient’s medical condition was last modified in the AHCCCS system. (The date the record was last modified in the database)
LOCATION_NAMECHARField not currently used by Hawaii
md5_hashVARCHARThe MD5 hash is built off all columns and is to be used with available primary and/or business keys included in the respective file. Is included to simplify any delta loading processes during file imports. row-by-row validation check using the hash bytes MD5 function to compare every character between the source and target tables. The primary objective, apart from confirming matching data, was to identify special characters that would cause issues with data loads.
MED_CNDCHARA code that describes a recipient's medical condition.
NOT_SRCCHARThe source that referred the information
PRI_AHCCCS_IDCHARThe identification number assigned to a recipient of services in the AHCCCS program

PMT_DATDATEThe date the voucher was paid
VOU_ID_NUMCHARACS issues checks when it makes capitation payments on behalf of MQD. Each detailed payment documented on the 820 Transaction has a Voucher Number. The same Voucher Number can be associated with information for multiple members on the 834 Enrollment Transaction. This association makes
it possible for receivers of both 820 and 834 Transactions to audit payments at the member level.
VOU_ID_PFXCHARThe prefix number of the voucher. (Voucher Identification Prefix)

TMSIS

FILE-HEADER-RECORD-IP
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
DATA-DICTIONARY-VERSIONData Dictionary Version918VarcharA data element to capture the version of the T-MSIS data dictionary that was used to build the file. Use the version number specified on the Cover Sheet of the data dictionary".
SUBMISSION-TRANSACTION-TYPESubmission Transaction Type1919CharA data element to identify the whether the transactions in the file are original submissions of the data, a resubmission of a previously submitted file, or corrections of edit rejects.
FILE-ENCODING-SPECIFICATIONFile Encoding Specification2022CharDenotes which supported file encoding standard was used to create the file.
DATA-MAPPING-DOCUMENT-VERSIONData Mapping Document Version2331CharIdentifies the version of the T-MSIS data mapping document used to build a state submission file. Use the version number specified on the title page of the data mapping document
FILE-NAMEFile Name3239CharA code to identify the subject area to which the records in its file relate. Each T-MSIS submission file should only
contain records for one subject area (i.e., Eligible, Third-party liability, Provider, Managed Care Plan Information,
Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State4041VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
DATE-FILE-CREATEDDate File Created4249DateThe date on which the file was created.
START-OF-TIME-PERIODStart of Time Period5057DateThis value must be the first day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span of the data in the file.
END-OF-TIME-PERIODEnd of Time Period5865dateThis value must be the last day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span.
FILE-STATUS-INDICATORFile Status Indicator6666CharA code to indicate whether the records in the file are test or production records.
SSN-INDICATORSSN Indicator6767NumIndicates whether the state uses the eligible person's social security number instead of an MSIS Identification Number as the unique, unchanging eligible person identifier. A state's SSN/Non-SSN designation on the eligibility file should match on the claims and third party liability
files.
TOT-REC-CNTTotal Record Count6878NumA count of all records in the file except for the file header record. This count will be used as a control total to help assure that the file did not become corrupted during transmission.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation83582VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
SEQUENCE-NUMBERSequence Number7982NumTo enable states to sequentially number files, when related, follow-on files are necessary (i.e. update files, replace files). This should begin with 1 for the
original Create submission type and be incremented by one for each Replacement or Update submission for the same reporting period and file type (subject
area).
CLAIM-HEADER-RECORD-IP
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
1115A-DEMONSTRATION-IND1115A Demonstration Indicator157157NumIn the claims files this data element indicates whether the claim or encounter was covered under the authority of an 1115A demonstration. In the Eligibility file, this data element indicates whether the individual participates in an 1115A demonstration.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date435442DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN72121NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
ADJUSTMENT-INDAdjustment Indicator158158VarcharIndicates the type of adjustment record.
ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODEAdjustment Reason Code159161NumClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a claim was paid differently than it was billed.
ADMISSION-DATEAdmission Date415422DateThe date on which the recipient was admitted to a hospital.
ADMISSION-HOURAdmission Hour423424NumThe hour of admission to a hospital.
ADMISSION-TYPEAdmission Type162162NumThe basic types of admission for Inpatient hospital stays and a code indicating the priority of this admission.
ADMITTING-DIAGNOSIS-CODEAdmitting Diagnosis Code183189NumThe ICD-9/10-CM Diagnosis Code provided at the time of admission by the physician. Since the Admitting Diagnosis is formulated before all tests and examinations are complete, it may be stated in the form of a problem or symptom and it may differ from any of the final diagnoses
recorded in the medical record.
ADMITTING-DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAGAdmitting Diagnosis Code Flag190190NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for the Admitting Diagnosis Code.
ADMITTING-PROV-NPI-NUMAdmitting Provider NPI Number10281037NumThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the doctor responsible for admitting a patient to a hospital or other inpatient health facility.
ADMITTING-PROV-NUMAdmitting Provider Number10381067VarcharThe Medicaid ID of the doctor responsible for admitting a patient to a hospital or other inpatient health facility.
ADMITTING-PROV-SPECIALTYAdmitting Provider Specialty10681069NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
ADMITTING-PROV-TAXONOMYAdmitting Provider Taxonomy10701081VarcharTaxonomic classification (code) for a given healthcare provider, as defined by the National Uniform Claim Committee.
ADMITTING-PROV-TYPEAdmitting Provider Type10821083NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
CHDR-ALLOWED-CHARGE-SRCALLOWED-CHARGE-SRCVarchar
BEGINNING-DATE-OF-SERVICEBeginning Date of Service19201927Date For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Coinsurance Date Paid12111218dateThe date the beneficiary paid the coinsurance amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-PAID-AMOUNTBeneficiary Coinsurance Paid Amount11981210NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their coinsurance for the covered services on the claim. Do not include coinsurance payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Copayment Date Paid12321239DateThe date the beneficiary paid the copayment amount.
BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Deductible Date Paid12531260DateThe date the beneficiary paid the deductible amount.
BILLING-PROV-NPI-NUMBilling Provider NPI Number10021011VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the billing entity responsible for billing a patient for healthcare services. The billing provider can also be servicing, referring, or prescribing provider. Can be admitting provider except for Long Term Care.
BILLING-PROV-NUMBilling Provider Number9721001VarcharA unique identification number assigned by the state to a provider or capitation plan. This data element should represent the entity billing for the service. For encounter records, if associated Type of Claim value equals 3, C, or W, then value must be the state identifier of the provider or entity
(billing or reporting) to the managed care plan.
BILLING-PROV-SPECIALTYBilling Provider Specialty10261027NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMYBilling Provider Taxonomy10121023VarcharThe taxonomy code for the institution billing for the beneficiary.
BILLING-PROV-TYPEBilling Provider Type10241025NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
BIRTH-WEIGHT-GRAMSBirth Weight Grams851859NumThe weight of a newborn at time of birth in grams (applicable to newborns only). The field is required when a claim involves a child birth.
CHDR-BMIBMINum
BORDER-STATE-INDBorder State Indicator11971197NumA code to indicate whether an individual received services or equipment across state borders. (The provider location is out of state, but for payment purposes the provider is treated as an in-state provider.)
CHECK-EFF-DATECheck Effective Date479486DateThe date a check is issued to the payee. In the case of electronic funds transfer, it is the date the transfer is made.
CHECK-NUMCheck Number464478NumThe check or electronic funds transfer number.
CLAIM-DENIED-INDICATORClaim Denied Indicator12611261NumAn indicator to identify a claim that the state refused pay in its entirety.
CLAIM-LINE-COUNTClaim Line Count665668NumThe total number of lines on the claim.
CLAIM-STATUSClaim Status456458VarcharThe health care claim status codes convey the status of an entire claim status codes from the 277 transaction set. Only report the claim status for the final, adjudicated claim.
CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORYClaim Status Category459461VarcharThe general category of the claim status (accepted, rejected, pended, finalized, additional information requested, etc.), which is then further detailed in the companion data element claim status.
COMBINED-BENE-COST-SHARING-PAID-AMOUNTCombined Beneficiary Cost Sharing Paid Amount19751987NumThe combined amounts the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible for the covered services on the claim. Only report this data element when the claim does not differentiate among
copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible payments made by the beneficiary. Do not include beneficiary cost sharing payments made by a third party/ies on behalf of the beneficiary.
COPAY-WAIVED-INDCopayment Waived Indicator12621262NumAn indicator signifying that the copay was discounted or waived by the provider (e.g., physician or hospital). Do not use to indicate administrative-level, Medicaid State Agency or Medicaid MCO copayment waived decisions.
CROSSOVER-INDICATORCrossover Indicator154154VarcharAn indicator specifying whether the claim is a crossover claim where a portion is paid by Medicare.
DATE-OF-BIRTHDate of Birth941948dateDate of birth of the individual to whom the services were provided. A patient's age should not be greater than 112 years.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-1Diagnosis Code 1191197VarcharThe primary/principal ICD-9/10-CM diagnosis code as reported on the claim.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-10Diagnosis Code 10272278VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-11Diagnosis Code 11281287VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".

adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".

adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".

adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".

passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".

passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing

passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing

adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-9Diagnosis Code 9263269VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-1Diagnosis Code Flag 1198198NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-10Diagnosis Code Flag 10279279NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-7Diagnosis Code Flag 7252252NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-8Diagnosis Code Flag 8261261NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-9Diagnosis Code Flag 9270270NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-1Diagnosis POA Flag 1199199VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-10Diagnosis POA Flag 10280280VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-11Diagnosis POA Flag 11289289VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably

POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably

*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.

POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably

*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-8Diagnosis POA Flag 8262262VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably

*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-9Diagnosis POA Flag 9271271VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-RELATED-GROUPDiagnosis Related Group299302NumA code representing the Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) that is applicable for the inpatient services being rendered. This field is required on FFS claims and encounters records in which diagnosis related groups are used to determine paid amounts.
DIAGNOSIS-RELATED-GROUP-INDDiagnosis Related Group Indicator303306VarcharAn indicator identifying the grouping algorithm used to assign Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) values. Values are generated by combining two types of information: Position 1-2, State/Group generating DRG: If state specific system, fill with two digit US postal code representation for state. If CMS Grouper, fill with 'HG'. If any Derived from TMSIS, fill with 'XX'. Position 3-4, fill with the number that represents the DRG version used (01-98). For example, 'HG15' would represent CMS Grouper version 15. If version is unknown, fill with '99'.
DISCHARGE-DATEDischarge Date425432DateThe date on which the recipient was discharged from a hospital.
DISCHARGE-HOURDischarge Hour433434NumThe hour of discharge from a hospital.
DRG-DESCRIPTIONDRG Description163182NumDescription of the associated state-specific DRG code. If using standard MS-DRG classification system, leave blank.
DRG-OUTLIER-AMTDRG Outlier Amount11241136NumThe additional payment on a claim that is associated with either a cost outlier or length of stay outlier.
Outlier payments compensate hospitals paid on a fixed amount per Medicare "diagnosis related group" discharge with extra dollars for patient stays that substantially exceed the typical requirements for patient stays in the same DRG category.
DRG-REL-WEIGHTDRG Relative Weight11371144NumThe relative weight for the DRG on the claim. Each year CMS assigns a relative weight to each DRG. These weights indicate the relative costs for treating patients during the prior year. The national average charge for each DRG is compared to the overall average. This ratio is published annually in the Federal Register for each DRG. A DRG
with a weight of 2.0000 means that charges were historically twice the average; a DRG with a weight of 0.5000 was half the average. This data element in T-MSIS is expected to capture the relative weight of the DRG in the state's system regardless of which DRG system the state uses.
ELIGIBLE-FIRST-NAMEEligible First Name910939CharThe first name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification Number will be used to associate a claim record
with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-LAST-NAMEEligible Last Name880909CharThe last name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification Number will be used to associate a claim record
with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-MIDDLE-INITEligible Middle Initial940940CharIndividual's middle initial; middle initial component of full name (e.g. First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name).
ENDING-DATE-OF-SERVICEEnding Date of Service19281935Date For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
FIXED-PAYMENT-INDFixed Payment Indicator617617NumThis indicator indicates that the reimbursement amount included on the claim is for a fixed payment. Fixed payments are made by the state to insurers or providers for premiums or eligible coverage, not for a particular service. For example, some states have Primary Care Case Management
programs where the state pays providers a monthly patient management fee of $3.50 for each eligible participant under their care. This fee is considered a fixed payment. It is very important for states to correctly identify fixed payments. Fixed payments do not have a defined "medical record"
associated with the payment; therefore, fixed payments are not subject to medical record request and medical record review.
FORCED-CLAIM-INDForced Claim Indicator669669NumIndicates if the claim was processed by forcing it through a manual override process.
FUNDING-CODEFunding Code618619CharA code to indicate the source of non-federal share funds.
FUNDING-SOURCE-NONFEDERAL-SHAREFunding Source Non-Federal Share620621NumA code to indicate the type of non-federal share used by the state to finance its expenditure to the provider. In the event of two sources, states are to report the portion which represents the largest proportion not funded by the Federal government.
HEALTH-HOME-ENTITY-NAMEHealth Home Entity Name12631312VarcharA free-form text field to indicate the health home program that authorized payment for the service on the claim or to identify the health home SPA in which an individual is enrolled. The name entered should be the name that the state uses to uniquely identify the team. A "Health Home Entity" can be a designated provider (e.g., physician, clinic,
behavioral health organization), a health team which links to a designated provider, or a health team (physicians, nurses, behavioral health professionals). Because an identification numbering schema has not been established, the entities' names are being used instead.
HEALTH-HOME-PROV-INDHealth Home Provider Indicator949949NumIndicates whether the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the Health Home care model to provide services for the beneficiary on the claim. Health home providers provide service for patients with chronic illnesses. States that do not specify an eligible individual's health home provider number, if applicable, should not
report claims that indicate the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the health home model.
HEALTH-HOME-PROVIDER-NPIHealth Home Provider NPI Number13681377VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the health home provider.
HEALTH-CARE-ACQUIRED-CONDITION-INDHealthcare Acquired Condition Indicator670670NumThis code indicates whether the claim has a Health Care Acquired Condition. For additional coding information refer to the following site :
https://www.cms.gov/Medicare/Medicare-Fee-for-Service-Payment/HospitalAcqCond/index.html?redirect=/hospitalacqcond/05_​Coding.asp#TopOfPage
MEDICAID-AMOUNT-PAID-DSHMedicaid Amount Paid DSH13551367NumThe amount included in the Total Medicaid Amount (CIP.002.114) that is attributable to a Disproportionate Share Hospital (DSH) payment, when the state makes DSH payments by claim.
MEDICAID-COV-INPATIENT-DAYSMedicaid Covered Inpatient Days658664NumThe number of days covered by Medicaid on this claim. For states that combine delivery/birth services on a single claim, include covered days for both the mother and the neonate in this field.
MEDICAID-PAID-DATEMedicaid Paid Date443450DateThe date Medicaid paid this claim or adjustment. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), the date the managed care organization paid the provider for the claim or adjustment.
MEDICARE-BENEFICIARY-IDENTIFIERMedicare Beneficiary Identifier13781389VarcharThe Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) is a randomly generated identifier used to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. It replaced the previously-used SSN-based Medicare HIC Number (HICN). To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the MBI
over the course of 2018 and 2019. Starting in 2020, the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries.
MEDICARE-COMB-DED-INDMedicare Combined Deductible Indicator622622NumCode indicating that the amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP on this claim toward the recipient's Medicare deductible was combined with their coinsurance amount because the amounts could not be separated.
MEDICARE-HIC-NUMMedicare HIC Number11451156NumThe Medicare HIC Number (HICN) is an identifier formerly used by SSA and CMS to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. For many beneficiaries, their SSN was a major component of their HICN. To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) over the course of 2018 and 2019. HICN continue to be used by Medicare for limited administrative purposes after 2019 but starting in 2020 the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries. HICN consists of two components: SSN &
alpha-suffix or (for Railroad IDs) prefix and ID (not always SSN based)
MEDICARE-PAID-AMTMedicare Paid Amount14021414NumThe amount paid by Medicare on this claim. For claims where Medicare payment is only available at the line level, report the sum of all the line level Medicare payment amounts at the header.
MEDICARE-REIM-TYPEMedicare Reimbursement Type638639NumA code to indicate the type of Medicare reimbursement.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number134153NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier. MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique 'key' value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods. See T-MSIS Guidance Document, 'CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers' for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the same MSIS Identification Number. https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
CHDR-NAT-HC-ENTITY-IDNATIONAL-HEALTH-CARE-ENTITY-IDVarchar
NON-COV-CHARGESNon-Covered Charges645657NumThe charges for inpatient care, which are not reimbursable by the primary payer. The non-covered charges do not refer to charges not covered for any other service.
NON-COV-DAYSNon-Covered Days640644NumThe number of days of inpatient care not covered by the payer for this sequence as qualified by the payer organization. The number of non-covered days does not refer to days not covered for any other service.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-01Occurrence Code 1671672VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-10Occurrence Code 10689690VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-02Occurrence Code 2673674VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-03Occurrence Code 3675676VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-04Occurrence Code 4677678VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-05Occurrence Code 5679680VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-06Occurrence Code 6681682VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-07Occurrence Code 7683684VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-08Occurrence Code 8685686VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-09Occurrence Code 9687688VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-01Occurrence Code Effective Date 1691698dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-10Occurrence Code Effective Date 10763770dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-02Occurrence Code Effective Date 2699706dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-03Occurrence Code Effective Date 3707714dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-04Occurrence Code Effective Date 4715722dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-05Occurrence Code Effective Date 5723730dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-06Occurrence Code Effective Date 6731738dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-07Occurrence Code Effective Date 7739746dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-08Occurrence Code Effective Date 8747754dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-09Occurrence Code Effective Date 9755762dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-01Occurrence Code End Date 1771778dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-10Occurrence Code End Date 10843850dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-02Occurrence Code End Date 2779786dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-03Occurrence Code End Date 3787794dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-04Occurrence Code End Date 4795802dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-05Occurrence Code End Date 5803810dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-06Occurrence Code End Date 6811818dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-07Occurrence Code End Date 7819826dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-08Occurrence Code End Date 8827834dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-09Occurrence Code End Date 9835842dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OPERATING-PROV-TAXONOMYOperating Provider Taxonomy13901401VarcharTaxonomic classification (code) for a given healthcare provider, as defined by the National Uniform Claim Committee.
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN2271NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
OTHER-INSURANCE-INDOther Insurance Indicator598598NumThe field denotes whether the insured party is covered under another insurance plan other than Medicare or Medicaid.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection599601NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
OUTLIER-CODEOutlier Code11571158NumThis code indicates the Type of Outlier Code or DRG Source. The field identifies two mutually exclusive conditions. The first, for PPS providers (codes 0, 1, and 2), classifies stays of exceptional cost or length (outliers). The second, for non-PPS providers (codes 6, 7, 8, and 9),
denotes the source for developing the DRG. https://www.resdac.org/cms-data/variables/medpar-drgoutlier-stay-code
OUTLIER-DAYSOutlier Days11591163NumThis field specifies the number of days paid as outliers under Prospective Payment System (PPS) and the days over the threshold for the DRG.
PATIENT-CONTROL-NUMPatient Control Number860879VarcharA patient's unique number assigned by the provider agency during claim submission, which identifies the client or the client's episode of service within the provider's system to facilitate retrieval of
individual financial and clinical records and posting of payment
PATIENT-STATUSPatient Status11641165NumA code indicating the patient's status as of the last day the claim covers. Values used are from UB-04. This is also referred to as patient discharge status. A valid list of codes can be purchased at https://www.nubc.org/license
PAYMENT-LEVEL-INDPayment Level Indicator637637NumThe field denotes whether the payment amount was determined at the claim header or line/detail level.
For claims where payment is NOT determined at the individual line level (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 1), the claim lines’ associated allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts are left blank and the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amount is reported at the header level only.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines and when applicable, cost-sharing and/or coordination of benefits were deducted from one or more specific line-level payment/allowed amounts (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 2), the allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts on the associated claim lines should sum to the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts reported on the claim header.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines but then cost sharing or coordination of benefits was deducted from the total paid/allowed amount at the header only (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 3), then the line-level paid amount (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) would be blank and line-level allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and header level total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts must all be populated but the line level allowed amounts are not expected to sum exactly to the header level total allowed.
For example, if a claim for an office visit and a procedure is assigned a separate line-level allowed amount for each line, but then at the header level a copay is deducted from the header-level total allowed and/or total paid amounts, then the sum of line-level allowed amounts may not be equal to the header-level total allowed amounts or correspond directly to the total paid amount. If the state cannot distinguish between the scenarios for value 1 and value 3, then value 1 can be used for all claims with only header-level total allowed/paid amounts.
PLAN-ID-NUMBERPlan ID Number625636VarcharA unique number assigned by the state which represents a distinct comprehensive managed care plan, prepaid health plan, primary care case management program, a program for all-inclusive care for the elderly entity, or other approved plans.
PROCEDURE-CODE-1Procedure Code 1307314VarcharA procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code1, Procedure Code Date-1, and Procedure Code Flag 1. The principal procedure is performed for definitive
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments. Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
PROCEDURE-CODE-2Procedure Code 2325332VarcharA procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code 1, Procedure Code 1, and Procedure Code Flag 1. The principal procedure is performed for definitive
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments. Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
PROCEDURE-CODE-3Procedure Code 3343350VarcharA procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code 1, Procedure Code Date 1, and Procedure Code Flag 1. The principal procedure is performed for definitive
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments. Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
PROCEDURE-CODE-4Procedure Code 4361368VarcharA procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code 1, Procedure Code Date 1, and Procedure Code Flag 1. The principal procedure is performed for definitive
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments. Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
PROCEDURE-CODE-5Procedure Code 5379386VarcharA procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code 1, Procedure Code Date 1, and Procedure Code Flag 1. The principal procedure is performed for definitive
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments. Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
PROCEDURE-CODE-6Procedure Code 6397404VarcharA procedure code based on ICD-9 and ICD-10 used by the state to identify the procedures performed during the hospital stay referenced by this claim. The principal procedure and related info should be recorded in Procedure Code 1, Procedure Code Date 1, and Procedure Code Flag 1. The principal procedure is performed for definitive
treatment rather than for diagnostic or exploratory purposes. It is closely related to either the principal diagnosis or to complications that arise during other treatments. Use Procedure Code 2 through Procedure Code 6 (and related data elements) to record secondary, tertiary, etc. procedures.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATE-1Procedure Code Date 1317324DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATE-2Procedure Code Date 2335342DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATE-3Procedure Code Date 3353360DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATE-4Procedure Code Date 4371378DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATE-5Procedure Code Date 5389396DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATE-6Procedure Code Date 6407414DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAG-1Procedure Code Flag 1315316NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAG-2Procedure Code Flag 2333334NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAG-3Procedure Code Flag 3351352NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAG-4Procedure Code Flag 4369370NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAG-5Procedure Code Flag 5387388NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAG-6Procedure Code Flag 6405406NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
CHDR-PROCEDURE-MOD-1PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-1Varchar
CHDR-PROCEDURE-MOD-2PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-2Varchar
CHDR-PROCEDURE-MOD-3PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-3Varchar
CHDR-PROCEDURE-MOD-4PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-4Varchar
CHDR-PROCEDURE-MOD-5PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-5Varchar
CHDR-PROCEDURE-MOD-6PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-6Varchar
PROGRAM-TYPEProgram Type623624NumA code to indicate special Medicaid program under which the service was provided.
PROV-LOCATION-IDProvider Location ID19151919VarcharA code to uniquely identify the geographic location where the provider's services were performed. The Provider Location Identifier values reported on Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim Header Segments must correspond to an active Provider Location Identifier
value on a Provider Location and Contact Info (PRV.003) segment. If a particular license (e.g., a physician's medical license) or provider identifier (e.g., an individual provider's NPI or SSN) is applicable to all of their servicing locations, value "000" (a string of exactly three zeros) can
be used in the PRV.004 or PRV.005, respectively, to represent all locations, however that location identifier must not be attributed to claims or provider bed type info.
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
REFERRING-PROV-NPI-NUMReferring Provider NPI Number11141123VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the provider who recommended the servicing provider to the patient.
REFERRING-PROV-NUMReferring Provider Number10841113VarcharA unique identification number assigned to a provider which identifies the physician or other provider who referred the patient. For physicians, this must be the individual's ID number, not a
group identification number. If the referring provider number is not available, but the physician's Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) ID is on the State file, then the State should use the DEA
ID for this data element.
CHDR-REF-PROV-SPECREFERRING-PROV-SPECIALTYVarchar
CHDR-REF-PROV-TAXONOMYREFERRING-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
CHDR-REF-PROV-TYPEREFERRING-PROV-TYPEVarchar
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-1Remittance Advice Remark Code 1487491VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-2Remittance Advice Remark Code 2492496VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-3Remittance Advice Remark Code 3497501VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-4Remittance Advice Remark Code 4502506VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
REMITTANCE-NUMRemittance Number11661195VarcharThe Remittance Advice Number is a sequential number that identifies the current Remittance Advice (RA) produced for a provider. The number is incremented by one each time a new RA is generated. The first five (5) positions are Julian date following a YYDDD format. The RA is the detailed
explanation of the reason for the payment amount.
SERVICE-TRACKING-PAYMENT-AMTService Tracking Payment Amount604616NumOn service tracking claims, the payment amount is the lump sum that cannot be attributed to any one beneficiary paid to the provider.
SERVICE-TRACKING-TYPEService Tracking Type602603NumA code to categorize service tracking claims. A "service tracking claim" is used to report lump sum payments that cannot be attributed to a single enrollee.
SOURCE-LOCATIONSource Location462463NumThe field denotes the claims payment system from which the claim was extracted.
SPLIT-CLAIM-INDSplit Claim Indicator11961196NumAn indicator that denotes that claims in excess of a pre-determined number of claim lines (threshold determined by the individual state) will be split during processing.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation14151914VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID122133NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State910VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
THIRD-PARTY-COINSURANCE-AMOUNT-PAIDThird Party Coinsurance Amount Paid13131325NumThe amount of money paid by a third party on behalf of the beneficiary towards coinsurance.
THIRD-PARTY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDThird Party Coinsurance Date Paid13261333DateThe date the third party paid the coinsurance amount
THIRD-PARTY-COPAYMENT-AMOUNT-PAIDThird Party Copayment Amount Paid13341346NumThe amount of money paid by a third party on behalf of the beneficiary towards copayment.
THIRD-PARTY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDThird Party Copayment Date Paid13471354DateThe date the third party paid the copayment amount.
TOT-ALLOWED-AMTTotal Allowed Amount520532NumThe claim header level maximum amount determined by the payer as being 'allowable' under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On FFS claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS. On managed care encounters the Allowed Amount is
determined by the managed care organization.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Coinsurance Liable Amount19491961NumThe total coinsurance amount on a claim that the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary coinsurance liability for covered service on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
copayment.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Liable Amount19361948NumThe total copayment amount on a claim that the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary copayment liability for covered service on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
copayment.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Paid Amount12191231NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment for the covered services on the claim. Do not include copayment payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Liable Amount19621974NumThe total deductible amount on a claim the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This amount is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary deductible liability for covered services on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
deductible.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Paid Amount12401252NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their deductible for the covered services on the claim. Do not include deductible payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
TOT-BILLED-AMTTotal Billed Amount507519NumThe total amount billed for this claim at the claim header level as submitted by the provider. For encounter records, when Type of Claim value is [ 3, C, or W ], then value must equal amount the provider billed to the managed care plan. Total Billed Amount is not expected on financial
transactions.
TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMTTotal Medicaid Paid Amount533545NumThe total amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim header level, which is the sum of the amounts paid by Medicaid or the managed care plan at the detail level for the claim.
TOT-MEDICARE-COINS-AMTTotal Medicare Coinsurance Amount559571NumThe total amount paid by the Medicaid/CHIP agency or a managed care plan towards the portion of the Medicare allowed charges that Medicare applied to coinsurance.
TOT-MEDICARE-DEDUCTIBLE-AMTTotal Medicare Deductible Amount546558NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP, on this claim at the claim header level, toward the beneficiary&#x27;s Medicare deductible. If the Medicare deductible amount can be identified separately from Medicare coinsurance payments, code that amount in this field. If the Medicare coinsurance and deductible payments cannot be separated, fill this field with the combined payment amount, code Medicare Combined Indicator a '1' and leave Total Medicare Coinsurance Amount unpopulated.
TOT-OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTTotal Other Insurance Amount585597NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
TOT-TPL-AMTTotal TPL Amount572584NumThird-party liability refers to the legal obligation of third parties, i.e., certain individuals, entities, or programs, to pay all or part of the expenditures for medical assistance furnished under a state plan. This is the total amount denoted at the claim header level paid by the third party.
CHDR-TOT-COPAY-AMTTOT-COPAY-AMTNum
TYPE-OF-BILLType of Bill452455NumA data element corresponding with UB-04 form locator FL4 that classifies the claim as to the type of facility (2nd digit), type of care (3rd digit) and the billing record&#x27;s sequence in the episode of care (4th digit). (Note that the 1st digit is always zero.)
TYPE-OF-CLAIMType of Claim451451VarcharA code to indicate what type of payment is covered in this claim.
TYPE-OF-HOSPITALType of Hospital155156NumThis code denotes the type of hospital on the claim (servicing facility).
CHDR-UND-DIR-PROV-NPIUNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-NPIVarcharUNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-NPI
CHDR-UND-DIR-PROV-TAXONUNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMYVarcharUNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMY
CHDR-UND-SUP-PROV-NPIUNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-NPIVarcharUNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-NPI
CHDR-UND-SUP-PROV-TAXONUNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMYVarcharUNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMY
WAIVER-IDWaiver ID952971VarcharField specifying the waiver or demonstration which authorized payment for a claim. These IDs must be the approved, full federal waiver ID number assigned during the state submission and CMS approval process. Waiver IDs should actually only be the
"core" part of the waiver IDs, without including suffixes for renewals or amendments.
WAIVER-TYPEWaiver Type950951NumA code for specifying waiver type under which the eligible individual is covered during the month and receiving services/under which claim is submitted.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
CLAIM-LINE-RECORD-IP
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State910VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number2241NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier.

MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

See T-MSIS Guidance Document, "CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers" for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the
same MSIS Identification Number.
https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN4291NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN92141NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
LINE-NUM-ORIGOriginal Line Number142144NumA unique number to identify the transaction line number that is being reported on the original claim.
LINE-NUM-ADJAdjustment Line Number145147NumA unique number to identify the transaction line number that identifies the line number on the adjustment claim.
LINE-ADJUSTMENT-INDLine Adjustment Indicator148148NumA code to indicate the type of adjustment record claim/encounter represents at claim detail level.
LINE-ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODELine Adjustment Reason Code149151VarcharClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a service line was paid differently than it was billed.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID152163NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
CLAIM-LINE-STATUSClaim Line Status164166VarcharThe claim line status codes from the 277 transaction set identify the status of a specific detail claim line rather than the entire claim. Only report the claim line for the final, adjudicated claim.
BEGINNING-DATE-OF-SERVICEBeginning Date of Service167174DateFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ENDING-DATE-OF-SERVICEEnding Date of Service175182Date For services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
REVENUE-CODERevenue Code183186VarcharA code which identifies a specific accommodation, ancillary service or billing calculation (as defined by UB-04 Billing
Manual). Revenue Code should be passed through to T-MSIS exactly as it was billed by the provider on the provider's
837I or UB-04 claim. It is only required on Inpatient, Long-Term Care and Other Fee for Service claims and managed
care encounters that have a valid Type of Bill value. It's not required on financial transactions or non-institutional claims.
IMMUNIZATION-TYPEImmunization Type187188NumThis field identifies the type of immunization provided in order to track additional detail not currently contained in
Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) codes.
REVENUE-CENTER-QUANTITY-ACTUALRevenue Center Quantity Actual189197NumOn facility claim entries, this field is to capture the actual service quantity by revenue code category, e.g., number of days in a particular type of accommodation, pints of blood, etc. However, when HCPCS codes are required for services, the units are equal to the number of times the procedure/service being reported was performed. For CLAIMOT claims/encounter records use Service Quantity Actual and CLAIMRX claims/encounter records use the Prescription Quantity Actual field
REVENUE-CENTER-QUANTITY-ALLOWEDRevenue Center Quantity Allowed198206NumOn facility claim entries, this field is to capture maximum allowable quantity by revenue code category, e.g., number of days in a particular type of accommodation, pints of blood, etc. However, when HCPCS codes are required for services, the units are equal to the number of times the procedure/service
being reported was performed. This field is only applicable when the service being billed can be quantified in discrete units, e.g., a number of visits or the number of units of a prescription/refill that were filled. For CLAIMOT claims/encounter records use Service Quantity Actual and CLAIMRX claims/encounter records use the Prescription Quantity Actual field
REVENUE-CHARGERevenue Charge207219NumThe total amount billed for the related Revenue Code. Total amount billed includes both covered and non-covered charges (as defined by UB-04 Billing Manual). For encounter records, Type of Claim = 3, C, or W, this field should be populated with the amount that the provider billed to the
managed care plan.
ALLOWED-AMTAllowed Amount220232NumThe maximum amount displayed at the claim line level as determined by the payer as being "allowable" under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On Fee for Service claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS (or PBM). On managed
care encounters the Allowed Amount is determined by the managed care organization.
CDTL-TPL-AMTTPL-AMTNum
MEDICAID-PAID-AMTMedicaid Paid Amount233245NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP agency or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim detail level.
