MEDICAID-FFS-EQUIVALENT-AMTMedicaid FFS Equivalent Amount246258NumThe amount that would have been paid had the services been provided on a Fee for Service basis.
BILLING-UNITBilling Unit259260NumUnit of billing that is used for billing services by the facility.
TYPE-OF-SERVICEType of Service261263NumA code to categorize the services provided to a Medicaid or CHIP enrollee.
SERVICING-PROV-NUMServicing Provider Number264293VarcharA unique number to identify the provider who treated the recipient. The Servicing Provider Number should be for the individual doctor who rendered the service. If "Servicing" provider and the "Billing" provider such as a sole-practitioner are the same then use the same number in both fields. The
value is conditional as its usage varies by state.
SERVICING-PROV-NPI-NUMServicing Provider NPI Number294303VarcharThe NPI of the health care professional who delivers or completes a particular medical service or non-surgical
procedure. The Servicing Provider NPI Number is required when rendering provider is different than the attending
provider and state or federal regulatory requirements call for a "combined claim" (i.e., a claim that includes both facility
and professional components). Examples are Medicaid clinic bills or critical access hospital claims.
CDTL-SERV-PROV-TAXONOMYSERVICING-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
SERVICING-PROV-TYPEServicing Provider Type304305NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
SERVICING-PROV-SPECIALTYServicing Provider Specialty306307NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
OPERATING-PROV-NPI-NUMOperating Provider NPI Number308317NumThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the provider who performed the surgical procedures on the beneficiary.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection318320NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
PROV-FACILITY-TYPEProvider Facility Type321329NumThe type of facility in which services on the claim were rendered. The Provider Facility Type code set is based on corresponding groups of HIPAA provider taxonomy codes.
BENEFIT-TYPEBenefit Type330332NumThe benefit category corresponding to the service reported on the claim or encounter record. Note: The code definitions in the valid value list originate from the Medicaid and CHIP Program Data System (MACPro) benefit type list. See Appendix H: Benefit Types
CMS-64-CATEGORY-FOR-FEDERAL-REIMBURSEMENTCMS 64 Category for Federal Reimbursement333334NumA code to indicate the Federal funding source for the payment.
XIX-MBESCBES-CATEGORY-OF-SERVICEXIX MBESCBES Category of Service335339VarcharA code indicating the category of service for the paid claim. The category of service is the line item from the CMS-64 form that states use to report their expenditures and request federal financial participation.
XXI-MBESCBES-CATEGORY-OF-SERVICEXXI MBESCBES Category of Service340342VarcharA code to indicate the category of service for the paid claim. The category of service is the line item from the CMS-21 form that states use to report their expenditures and request federal financial participation.
OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTOther Insurance Amount343355NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation356855VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
NDC-QUANTITYNDC Quantity884894NumThis field is to capture the actual quantity of the National Drug Code being prescribed on the claim.
HCPCS-RATEHCPCS Rate856869VarcharThis data element is expected to capture data from the HIPAA 837I claim loop 2400 SV206 or UB-04 FL 44.
NATIONAL-DRUG-CODENational Drug Code870881VarcharA code following the National Drug Code format indicating the drug, device, or medical supply covered by this claim.
NDC-UNIT-OF-MEASURENDC Unit of Measure882883VarcharA code to indicate the basis by which the quantity of the National Drug Code is expressed.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date895902DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
SELF-DIRECTION-TYPESelf-Direction Type903905NumThis data element is not applicable to this file type.
PRE-AUTHORIZATION-NUMPreauthorization Number906923VarcharA number, code or other value that indicates the services provided on this claim have been authorized by the payee or other service organization, or that a referral for services has been approved. (Also referred to as a Prior Authorization or Referral Number).
IHS-SERVICE-INDIHS Service Indicator924924NumTo indicate Services received by Medicaid-eligible individuals who are American Indian or Alaska Native (AI/AN) through facilities of the Indian Health Service (IHS), whether operated by IHS or by Tribes.
FILE-HEADER-RECORD-LT
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
DATA-DICTIONARY-VERSIONData Dictionary Version918VarcharA data element to capture the version of the T-MSIS data dictionary that was used to build the file. Use the version number specified on the Cover Sheet of the data dictionary".
SUBMISSION-TRANSACTION-TYPESubmission Transaction Type1919CharA data element to identify the whether the transactions in the file are original submissions of the data, a resubmission of a previously submitted file, or corrections of edit rejects.
FILE-ENCODING-SPECIFICATIONFile Encoding Specification2022CharDenotes which supported file encoding standard was used to create the file.
DATA-MAPPING-DOCUMENT-VERSIONData Mapping Document Version2331CharIdentifies the version of the T-MSIS data mapping document used to build a state submission file. Use the version number specified on the title page of the data mapping document
FILE-NAMEFile Name3239CharA code to identify the subject area to which the records in its file relate. Each T-MSIS submission file should only
contain records for one subject area (i.e., Eligible, Third-party liability, Provider, Managed Care Plan Information,
Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State4041NumA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
DATE-FILE-CREATEDDate File Created4249DateThe date on which the file was created.
START-OF-TIME-PERIODStart of Time Period5057DateThis value must be the first day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span of the data in the file.
END-OF-TIME-PERIODEnd of Time Period5865DateThis value must be the last day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span.
FILE-STATUS-INDICATORFile Status Indicator6666CharA code to indicate whether the records in the file are test or production records.
SSN-INDICATORSSN Indicator6767NumIndicates whether the state uses the eligible person's social security number instead of an MSIS Identification Number as the unique, unchanging eligible person identifier. A state's SSN/Non-SSN designation on the eligibility file should match on the claims and third party liability
files.
TOT-REC-CNTTotal Record Count6878NumA count of all records in the file except for the file header record. This count will be used as a control total to help assure that the file did not become corrupted during transmission.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation83582VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
SEQUENCE-NUMBERSequence Number7982NumTo enable states to sequentially number files, when related, follow-on files are necessary (i.e. update files, replace files). This should begin with 1 for the
original Create submission type and be incremented by one for each Replacement or Update submission for the same reporting period and file type (subject
area).
CLAIM-HEADER-RECORD-LT
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
SUBMIT+F279+D277:D+D277:D281Submitting State910NumA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN2271NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN72121NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID122133NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number134153NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier. MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique 'key' value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods. See T-MSIS Guidance Document, 'CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers' for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the same MSIS Identification Number. https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
CROSSOVER-INDICATORCrossover Indicator154154VarcharAn indicator specifying whether the claim is a crossover claim where a portion is paid by Medicare.
1115A-DEMONSTRATION-IND1115A Demonstration Indicator155155NumIn the claims files this data element indicates whether the claim or encounter was covered under the authority of an 1115A demonstration. In the Eligibility file, this data element indicates whether the individual participates in an 1115A demonstration.
ADJUSTMENT-INDAdjustment Indicator156156VarcharIndicates the type of adjustment record.
ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODEAdjustment Reason Code157159VarcharClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a claim was paid differently than it was billed.
ADMITTING-DIAGNOSIS-CODEAdmitting Diagnosis Code160166NumThe ICD-9/10-CM Diagnosis Code provided at the time of admission by the physician.
ADMITTING-DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAGAdmitting Diagnosis Code Flag167167NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for the Admitting Diagnosis Code.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-1Diagnosis Code 1168174VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-1Diagnosis Code Flag 1175175NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-1Diagnosis POA Flag 1176176VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-2Diagnosis Code 2177183VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-2Diagnosis Code Flag 2184184NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-2Diagnosis POA Flag 2185185NumA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-3Diagnosis Code 3186192VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-3Diagnosis Code Flag 3193193NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-3Diagnosis POA Flag 3194194VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-4Diagnosis Code 4195201VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-4Diagnosis Code Flag 4202202NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-4Diagnosis POA Flag 4203203VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-5Diagnosis Code 5204210VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-5Diagnosis Code Flag 5211211NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-5Diagnosis POA Flag 5212212VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
ADMISSION-DATEAdmission Date213220DateThe date on which the recipient was admitted to a psychiatric or long-term care facility.
ADMISSION-HOURAdmission Hour221222NumThe time of admission to a psychiatric or long-term care facility.
DISCHARGE-DATEDischarge Date223230DateThe date on which the recipient was discharged from a psychiatric or long-term care facility.
DISCHARGE-HOURDischarge Hour231232NumThe time of discharge from a psychiatric or long-term care facility.
BEGINNING-DATE-OF-SERVICEBeginning Date of Service233240DateFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ENDING-DATE-OF-SERVICEEnding Date of Service241248DateFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date249256DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
MEDICAID-PAID-DATEMedicaid Paid Date257264DateThe date Medicaid paid this claim or adjustment. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), the date the managed care organization paid the provider for the claim or adjustment.
TYPE-OF-CLAIMType of Claim265265VarcharA code to indicate what type of payment is covered in this claim.
TYPE-OF-BILLType of Bill266269NumA data element corresponding with UB-04 form locator FL4 that classifies the claim as to the type of facility (2nd digit), type of care (3rd digit) and the billing record&#x27;s sequence in the episode of care (4th digit). (Note that the 1st digit is always zero.)
CLAIM-STATUSClaim Status270272VarcharThe health care claim status codes convey the status of an entire claim status codes from the 277 transaction set. Only report the claim status for the final, adjudicated claim.
CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORYClaim Status Category273275VarcharThe general category of the claim status (accepted, rejected, pended, finalized, additional information requested, etc.) from the 277 transaction set which is then further detailed in the companion data element claim status.
SOURCE-LOCATIONSource Location276277NumThe field denotes the claims payment system from which the claim was extracted.
CHECK-NUMCheck Number278292NumThe check or electronic funds transfer number.
CHECK-EFF-DATECheck Effective Date293300DateThe date a check is issued to the payee. In the case of electronic funds transfer, it is the date the transfer is made.
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-1Remittance Advice Remark Code 1301305VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-2Remittance Advice Remark Code 2306310VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-3Remittance Advice Remark Code 3311315VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-4Remittance Advice Remark Code 4316320VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
TOT-BILLED-AMTTotal Billed Amount321333NumThe total amount billed for this claim at the claim header level as submitted by the provider. For encounter records, when Type of Claim value is [ 3, C, or W ], then value must equal amount the provider billed to the managed care plan. Total Billed Amount is not expected on financial
transactions.
TOT-ALLOWED-AMTTotal Allowed Amount334346NumThe claim header level maximum amount determined by the payer as being 'allowable' under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On FFS claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS. On managed care encounters the Allowed Amount is
determined by the managed care organization.
TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMTTotal Medicaid Paid Amount347359NumThe total amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim header level, which is the sum of the amounts paid by Medicaid or the managed care plan at the detail level for the claim.
TOT-COPAY-AMTNum
TOT-MEDICARE-DEDUCTIBLE-AMTTotal Medicare Deductible Amount360372NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP, on this claim at the claim header level, toward the beneficiary&#x27;s Medicare deductible. If the Medicare deductible amount can be identified separately from Medicare coinsurance payments, code that amount in this field. If the Medicare coinsurance and deductible payments cannot be separated, fill this field with the combined payment amount, code Medicare Combined Indicator a '1' and leave Total Medicare Coinsurance Amount unpopulated.
TOT-MEDICARE-COINS-AMTTotal Medicare Coinsurance Amount373385NumThe total amount paid by the Medicaid/CHIP agency or a managed care plan towards the portion of the Medicare allowed charges that Medicare applied to coinsurance.
TOT-TPL-AMTTotal TPL Amount386398NumThird-party liability refers to the legal obligation of third parties, i.e., certain individuals, entities, or programs, to pay all or part of the expenditures for medical assistance furnished under a state plan. This is the total amount denoted at the claim header level paid by the third party.
TOT-OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTTotal Other Insurance Amount399411NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
OTHER-INSURANCE-INDOther Insurance Indicator412412NumThe field denotes whether the insured party is covered under another insurance plan other than Medicare or Medicaid.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection413415NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
SERVICE-TRACKING-TYPEService Tracking Type416417NumA code to categorize service tracking claims. A "service tracking claim" is used to report lump sum payments that cannot be attributed to a single enrollee.
SERVICE-TRACKING-PAYMENT-AMTService Tracking Payment Amount418430NumOn service tracking claims, the payment amount is the lump sum that cannot be attributed to any one beneficiary paid to the provider.
FIXED-PAYMENT-INDFixed Payment Indicator431431NumThis indicator indicates that the reimbursement amount included on the claim is for a fixed payment. Fixed payments are made by the state to insurers or providers for premiums or eligible coverage, not for a particular service. For example, some states have Primary Care Case Management
programs where the state pays providers a monthly patient management fee of $3.50 for each eligible participant under their care. This fee is considered a fixed payment. It is very important for states to correctly identify fixed payments. Fixed payments do not have a defined "medical record"
associated with the payment; therefore, fixed payments are not subject to medical record request and medical record review.
FUNDING-CODEFunding Code432433CharA code to indicate the source of non-federal share funds.
FUNDING-SOURCE-NONFEDERAL-SHAREFunding Source Non-Federal Share434435NumA code to indicate the type of non-federal share used by the state to finance its expenditure to the provider. In the event of two sources, states are to report the portion which represents the largest proportion not funded by the Federal government.
MEDICARE-COMB-DED-INDMedicare Combined Deductible Indicator436436NumCode indicating that the amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP on this claim toward the recipient's Medicare deductible was combined with their coinsurance amount because the amounts could not be separated.
PROGRAM-TYPEProgram Type437438NumA code to indicate special Medicaid program under which the service was provided.
PLAN-ID-NUMBERPlan ID Number439450VarcharA unique number assigned by the state which represents a distinct comprehensive managed care plan, prepaid health plan, primary care case management program, a program for all-inclusive care for the elderly entity, or other approved plans.
NATIONAL-HEALTH-CARE-ENTITY-IDVarchar
PAYMENT-LEVEL-INDPayment Level Indicator451451NumThe field denotes whether the payment amount was determined at the claim header or line/detail level.
For claims where payment is NOT determined at the individual line level (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 1), the claim lines’ associated allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts are left blank and the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amount is reported at the header level only.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines and when applicable, cost-sharing and/or coordination of benefits were deducted from one or more specific line-level payment/allowed amounts (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 2), the allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts on the associated claim lines should sum to the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts reported on the claim header.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines but then cost sharing or coordination of benefits was deducted from the total paid/allowed amount at the header only (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 3), then the line-level paid amount (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) would be blank and line-level allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and header level total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts must all be populated but the line level allowed amounts are not expected to sum exactly to the header level total allowed.
For example, if a claim for an office visit and a procedure is assigned a separate line-level allowed amount for each line, but then at the header level a copay is deducted from the header-level total allowed and/or total paid amounts, then the sum of line-level allowed amounts may not be equal to the header-level total allowed amounts or correspond directly to the total paid amount. If the state cannot distinguish between the scenarios for value 1 and value 3, then value 1 can be used for all claims with only header-level total allowed/paid amounts.
MEDICARE-REIM-TYPEMedicare Reimbursement Type452453NumA code to indicate the type of Medicare reimbursement.
NON-COV-DAYSNon-Covered Days454458NumThe number of days of institutional long-term care not covered by the payer for this sequence as qualified by the payer organization. The number of non-covered days does not refer to days not covered for any other service.
NON-COV-CHARGESNon-Covered Charges459471NumThe charges for institutional long-term care, which are not reimbursable by the primary payer. The non-covered charges do not refer to charges not covered for any other service.
MEDICAID-COV-INPATIENT-DAYSMedicaid Covered Inpatient Days472476NumThe number of inpatient psychiatric days covered by Medicaid on this claim.
CLAIM-LINE-COUNTClaim Line Count477480NumThe total number of lines on the claim.
FORCED-CLAIM-INDForced Claim Indicator481481NumIndicates if the claim was processed by forcing it through a manual override process.
HEALTH-CARE-ACQUIRED-CONDITION-INDHealthcare Acquired Condition Indicator482482NumThis code indicates whether the claim has a Health Care Acquired Condition. For additional coding information refer to the following site :
https://www.cms.gov/Medicare/Medicare-Fee-for-Service-Payment/HospitalAcqCond/index.html?redirect=/hospitalacqcond/05_​Coding.asp#TopOfPage
OCCURRENCE-CODE-01Occurrence Code 1483484VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-02Occurrence Code 2485486VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-03Occurrence Code 3487488VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-04Occurrence Code 4489490VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-05Occurrence Code 5491492VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-06Occurrence Code 6493494VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-07Occurrence Code 7495496VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-08Occurrence Code 8497498VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-09Occurrence Code 9499500VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-10Occurrence Code 10501502VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-01Occurrence Code Effective Date 1503510DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-02Occurrence Code Effective Date 2511518DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-03Occurrence Code Effective Date 3519526DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-04Occurrence Code Effective Date 4527534DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-05Occurrence Code Effective Date 5535542DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-06Occurrence Code Effective Date 6543550DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-07Occurrence Code Effective Date 7551558DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-08Occurrence Code Effective Date 8559566DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-09Occurrence Code Effective Date 9567574DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-10Occurrence Code Effective Date 10575582DateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-01Occurrence Code End Date 1583590DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-02Occurrence Code End Date 2591598DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-03Occurrence Code End Date 3599606DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-04Occurrence Code End Date 4607614DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-05Occurrence Code End Date 5615622DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-06Occurrence Code End Date 6623630DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-07Occurrence Code End Date 7631638DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-08Occurrence Code End Date 8639646DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-09Occurrence Code End Date 9647654DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-10Occurrence Code End Date 10655662DateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
PATIENT-CONTROL-NUMPatient Control Number663682NumA patient's unique number assigned by the provider agency during claim submission, which identifies the client or the client's episode of service within the provider's system to facilitate retrieval of individual financial and clinical records and posting of payment
ELIGIBLE-LAST-NAMEEligible Last Name683712CharThe last name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it
appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification
Number will be used to associate a claim record with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-FIRST-NAMEEligible First Name713742CharThe first name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification Number will be used to associate a claim record
with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-MIDDLE-INITEligible Middle Initial743743CharIndividual's middle initial; middle initial component of full name (e.g. First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name).
DATE-OF-BIRTHDate of Birth744751DateAn individual's date of birth.
HEALTH-HOME-PROV-INDHealth Home Provider Indicator752752NumIndicates whether the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the Health Home care model to provide services for the beneficiary on the claim. Health home providers provide service for patients with chronic illnesses. States that do not specify an eligible individual's health home provider number, if applicable, should not
report claims that indicate the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the health home model.
WAIVER-TYPEWaiver Type753754NumA code for specifying waiver type under which the eligible individual is covered during the month and receiving services/under which claim is submitted.
WAIVER-IDWaiver ID755774VarcharField specifying the waiver or demonstration which authorized payment for a claim. These IDs must be the approved, full federal waiver ID number assigned during the state submission and CMS approval process. Waiver IDs should actually only be the
"core" part of the waiver IDs, without including suffixes for renewals or amendments.
BILLING-PROV-NUMBilling Provider Number775804VarcharA unique identification number assigned by the state to a provider or capitation plan. This data element should represent the entity billing for the service. For encounter records, if associated Type of Claim value equals 3, C, or W, then value must be the state identifier of the provider or entity
(billing or reporting) to the managed care plan.
BILLING-PROV-NPI-NUMBilling Provider NPI Number805814VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the billing entity responsible for billing a patient for healthcare services. The billing provider can also be servicing, referring, or prescribing provider. Can be admitting provider except for Long Term Care.
BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMYBilling Provider Taxonomy815826VarcharThe taxonomy code for the institution billing for the beneficiary.
BILLING-PROV-TYPEBilling Provider Type827828NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
BILLING-PROV-SPECIALTYBilling Provider Specialty829830NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
REFERRING-PROV-NUMReferring Provider Number831860VarcharA unique identification number assigned to a provider which identifies the physician or other provider who referred the patient. For physicians, this must be the individual's ID number, not a
group identification number. If the referring provider number is not available, but the physician's Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) ID is on the State file, then the State should use the DEA
ID for this data element.
REFERRING-PROV-NPI-NUMReferring Provider NPI Number861870VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the provider who recommended the servicing provider to the patient.
REFERRING-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
REFERRING-PROV-TYPEVarchar
REFERRING-PROV-SPECIALTYVarchar
MEDICARE-HIC-NUMMedicare HIC Number871882NumThe Medicare HIC Number (HICN) is an identifier formerly used by SSA and CMS to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. For many beneficiaries, their SSN was a major component of their HICN. To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) over the course of 2018 and 2019. HICN continue to be used by Medicare for limited administrative purposes after 2019 but starting in 2020 the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries. HICN consists of two components: SSN &
alpha-suffix or (for Railroad IDs) prefix and ID (not always SSN based)
PATIENT-STATUSPatient Status883884NumA code indicating the patient's status as of the last day the claim covers. Values used are from UB-04. This is also referred to as patient discharge status. A valid list of codes can be purchased at https://www.nubc.org/license
BMINum
REMITTANCE-NUMRemittance Number885914VarcharThe Remittance Advice Number is a sequential number that identifies the current Remittance Advice (RA) produced for a provider. The number is incremented by one each time a new RA is generated. The first five (5) positions are Julian date following a YYDDD format. The RA is the detailed
explanation of the reason for the payment amount.
LTC-RCP-LIAB-AMTLTC RCP Liability Amount915927NumThe total amount paid by the patient for services where they are required to use their personal funds to cover part of their care before Medicaid funds can be utilized.
DAILY-RATEDaily Rate928934NumThe amount a policy will pay per day for a covered service.
ICF-IID-DAYSICF IID Days935939NumThe number of days of intermediate care for individuals with an intellectual disability that were paid for in whole or in part by Medicaid. If value exceeds 99998 days, code as 99998. (e.g., code 100023 as 99998).
LEAVE-DAYSLeave Days940944NumThe number of days, during the period covered by Medicaid, on which the patient did not reside in the long term care facility.
NURSING-FACILITY-DAYSNursing Facility Days945949NumThe number of days of nursing care included in this claim that were paid for, in whole or in part, by Medicaid. Includes days during which nursing facility received partial payment for holding a bed during patient leave days. If value exceeds 99998 days, code as 99998.
SPLIT-CLAIM-INDSplit Claim Indicator950950NumAn indicator that denotes that claims in excess of a pre-determined number of claim lines (threshold determined by the individual state) will be split during processing.
BORDER-STATE-INDBorder State Indicator951951NumA code to indicate whether an individual received services or equipment across state borders. (The provider location is out of state, but for payment purposes the provider is treated as an in-state provider.)
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Coinsurance Paid Amount952964NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their coinsurance for the covered services on the claim. Do not include coinsurance payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Coinsurance Date Paid965972DateThe date the beneficiary paid the coinsurance amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Paid Amount973985NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment for the covered services on the claim. Do not include copayment payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary..
BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Copayment Date Paid986993DateThe date the beneficiary paid the copayment amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Paid Amount9941006NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment for the covered services on the claim. Do not include copayment payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Deductible Date Paid10071014DateThe date the beneficiary paid the deductible amount.
CLAIM-DENIED-INDICATORClaim Denied Indicator10151015NumAn indicator to identify a claim that the state refused pay in its entirety.
COPAY-WAIVED-INDCopayment Waived Indicator10161016NumAn indicator signifying that the copay was discounted or waived by the provider (e.g., physician or hospital). Do not use to indicate administrative-level, Medicaid State Agency or Medicaid MCO copayment waived decisions.
HEALTH-HOME-ENTITY-NAMEHealth Home Entity Name10171066VarcharA free-form text field to indicate the health home program that authorized payment for the service on the claim or to identify the health home SPA in which an individual is enrolled. The name entered should be the name that the state uses to uniquely identify the team. A "Health Home Entity" can be a designated provider (e.g., physician, clinic,
behavioral health organization), a health team which links to a designated provider, or a health team (physicians, nurses, behavioral health professionals). Because an identification numbering schema has not been established, the entities' names are being used instead.
THIRD-PARTY-COINSURANCE-AMOUNT-PAIDThird Party Coinsurance Amount Paid10671079NumThe amount of money paid by a third party on behalf of the beneficiary towards coinsurance.
THIRD-PARTY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDThird Party Coinsurance Date Paid10801087DateThe date the third party paid the coinsurance amount
THIRD-PARTY-COPAYMENT-AMOUNT-PAIDThird Party Copayment Amount Paid10881100NumThe amount of money paid by a third party on behalf of the beneficiary towards copayment.
THIRD-PARTY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDThird Party Copayment Date Paid11011108DateThe date the third party paid the copayment amount.
HEALTH-HOME-PROVIDER-NPIHealth Home Provider NPI Number11091120VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the health home provider.
MEDICARE-BENEFICIARY-IDENTIFIERMedicare Beneficiary Identifier11211132VarcharThe Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) is a randomly generated identifier used to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. It replaced the previously-used SSN-based Medicare HIC Number (HICN). To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the MBI
over the course of 2018 and 2019. Starting in 2020, the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries.
UNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-NPIVarchar
UNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
UNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-NPIVarchar
UNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation12021701VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
ADMITTING-PROV-NPI-NUMAdmitting Provider NPI Number11331142NumThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the doctor responsible for admitting a patient to a hospital or other inpatient health facility.
ADMITTING-PROV-NUMAdmitting Provider Number11431172VarcharThe Medicaid ID of the doctor responsible for admitting a patient to a hospital or other inpatient health facility.
ADMITTING-PROV-SPECIALTYAdmitting Provider Specialty11731174NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
ADMITTING-PROV-TAXONOMYAdmitting Provider Taxonomy11751186VarcharTaxonomic classification (code) for a given healthcare provider, as defined by the National Uniform Claim Committee.
ADMITTING-PROV-TYPEAdmitting Provider Type11871188NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
MEDICARE-PAID-AMTMedicare Paid Amount11891201NumThe amount paid by Medicare on this claim. For claims where Medicare payment is only available at the line level, report the sum of all the line level Medicare payment amounts at the header.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
PROV-LOCATION-IDProvider Location ID17021706VarcharA code to uniquely identify the geographic location where the provider's services were performed. The Provider Location Identifier values reported on Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim Header Segments must correspond to an active Provider Location Identifier
value on a Provider Location and Contact Info (PRV.003) segment. If a particular license (e.g., a physician's medical license) or provider identifier (e.g., an individual provider's NPI or SSN) is applicable to all of their servicing locations, value "000" (a string of exactly three zeros) can
be used in the PRV.004 or PRV.005, respectively, to represent all locations, however that location identifier must not be attributed to claims or provider bed type info.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Liable Amount17071719NumThe total copayment amount on a claim that the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary copayment liability for covered service on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
copayment.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Coinsurance Liable Amount17201732NumThe total coinsurance amount on a claim that the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary coinsurance liability for covered service on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
copayment.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Liable Amount17331745NumThe total deductible amount on a claim the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This amount is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary deductible liability for covered services on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
deductible.
COMBINED-BENE-COST-SHARING-PAID-AMOUNTCombined Beneficiary Cost Sharing Paid Amount17461758NumThe combined amounts the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible for the covered services on the claim. Only report this data element when the claim does not differentiate among
copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible payments made by the beneficiary. Do not include beneficiary cost sharing payments made by a third party/ies on behalf of the beneficiary.
CLAIM-LINE-RECORD-LT
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State910VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number2241NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier.

MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

See T-MSIS Guidance Document, "CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers" for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the
same MSIS Identification Number.
https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN4291NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN92141NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
LINE-NUM-ORIGOriginal Line Number142144NumA unique number to identify the transaction line number that is being reported on the original claim.
LINE-NUM-ADJAdjustment Line Number145147NumA unique number to identify the transaction line number that identifies the line number on the adjustment claim.
LINE-ADJUSTMENT-INDLine Adjustment Indicator148148NumA code to indicate the type of adjustment record claim/encounter represents at claim detail level.
LINE-ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODELine Adjustment Reason Code149151VarcharClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a service line was paid differently than it was billed.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID152163NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
CLAIM-LINE-STATUSClaim Line Status164166VarcharThe claim line status codes from the 277 transaction set identify the status of a specific detail claim line rather than the entire claim. Only report the claim line for the final, adjudicated claim.
BEGINNING-DATE-OF-SERVICEBeginning Date of Service167174NumFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ENDING-DATE-OF-SERVICEEnding Date of Service175182DateFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
REVENUE-CODERevenue Code183186VarcharA code which identifies a specific accommodation, ancillary service or billing calculation (as defined by UB-04 Billing
Manual). Revenue Code should be passed through to T-MSIS exactly as it was billed by the provider on the provider's
837I or UB-04 claim. It is only required on Inpatient, Long-Term Care and Other Fee for Service claims and managed
care encounters that have a valid Type of Bill value. It's not required on financial transactions or non-institutional claims.
IMMUNIZATION-TYPEImmunization Type187188Num[No longer essential - Both data element and associated requirement(s); preserved for file submission integrity. See Data Dictionary v2.3 for specific definition and coding requirement description(s).]
REVENUE-CENTER-QUANTITY-ACTUALRevenue Center Quantity Actual189197NumOn facility claim entries, this field is to capture the actual service quantity by revenue code category, e.g., number of days in a particular type of accommodation, pints of blood, etc. However, when HCPCS codes are required for services, the units are equal to the number of times the procedure/service being reported was performed. For CLAIMOT claims/encounter records use Service Quantity Actual and CLAIMRX claims/encounter records use the Prescription Quantity Actual field
REVENUE-CENTER-QUANTITY-ALLOWEDRevenue Center Quantity Allowed198206NumOn facility claim entries, this field is to capture maximum allowable quantity by revenue code category, e.g., number of days in a particular type of accommodation, pints of blood, etc. However, when HCPCS codes are required for services, the units are equal to the number of times the procedure/service being reported was performed. This field is only applicable when the service being billed can be quantified in discrete units, e.g., a number of visits or the number of units of a prescription/refill that were filled. For CLAIMOT claims/encounter records use Service Quantity Actual and CLAIMRX claims/encounter records use the Prescription Quantity Actual field
REVENUE-CHARGE Revenue Charge207219NumThe total amount billed for the related Revenue Code. Total amount billed includes both covered and non-covered charges (as defined by UB-04 Billing Manual). For encounter records, Type of Claim = 3, C, or W, this field should be populated with the amount that the provider billed to the
managed care plan.
ALLOWED-AMTAllowed Amount220232NumThe maximum amount displayed at the claim line level as determined by the payer as being "allowable" under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On Fee for Service claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS (or PBM). On managed
care encounters the Allowed Amount is determined by the managed care organization.
TPL-AMTTPL Amount233245NumThird-party liability refers to the legal obligation of third parties, i.e., certain individuals, entities, or programs, to pay all or part of the expenditures for medical assistance furnished under a state plan. This is the total amount denoted at the claim detail level paid by the third party.
OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTOther Insurance Amount246258NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
MEDICAID-PAID-AMTMedicaid Paid Amount259271NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP agency or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim detail level.
MEDICAID-FFS-EQUIVALENT-AMTMedicaid FFS Equivalent Amount272284NumThe amount that would have been paid had the services been provided on a Fee for Service basis.
BILLING-UNITBilling Unit285286NumUnit of billing that is used for billing services by the facility.
TYPE-OF-SERVICEType of Service287289NumA code to categorize the services provided to a Medicaid or CHIP enrollee.
SERVICING-PROV-NUMServicing Provider Number290319VarcharA unique number to identify the provider who treated the recipient. The Servicing Provider Number should be for the individual doctor who rendered the service. If "Servicing" provider and the "Billing" provider such as a sole-practitioner are the same then use the same number in both fields. The
value is conditional as its usage varies by state.
SERVICING-PROV-NPI-NUMServicing Provider NPI Number320329VarcharThe NPI of the health care professional who delivers or completes a particular medical service or non-surgical procedure. The Servicing Provider NPI Number is required when rendering provider is different than the attending provider and state or federal regulatory requirements call for
a "combined claim" (i.e., a claim that includes both facility and professional components). Examples are Medicaid clinic bills or critical access hospital claims.
SERVICING-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
SERVICING-PROV-TYPEServicing Provider Type330331NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
SERVICING-PROV-SPECIALTYServicing Provider Specialty332333NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection334336NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
BENEFIT-TYPEBenefit Type337339NumThe benefit category corresponding to the service reported on the claim or encounter record. Note: The code definitions in the valid value list originate from the Medicaid and CHIP Program Data System (MACPro) benefit type list. See Appendix H: Benefit Types
CMS-64-CATEGORY-FOR-FEDERAL-REIMBURSEMENTCMS 64 Category for Federal Reimbursement340341NumA code to indicate the Federal funding source for the payment.
PROV-FACILITY-TYPEProvider Facility Type342350NumThe type of facility in which services on the claim were rendered. The Provider Facility Type code set is based on corresponding groups of HIPAA provider taxonomy codes.
XIX-MBESCBES-CATEGORY-OF-SERVICEXIX MBESCBES Category of Service351355VarcharA code indicating the category of service for the paid claim. The category of service is the line item from the CMS-64 form that states use to report their expenditures and request federal financial participation.
XXI-MBESCBES-CATEGORY-OF-SERVICEXXI MBESCBES Category of Service356358VarcharA code to indicate the category of service for the paid claim. The category of service is the line item from the CMS-21 form that states use to report their expenditures and request federal financial participation.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation359858VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
NATIONAL-DRUG-CODENational Drug Code859870VarcharA code following the National Drug Code format indicating the drug, device, or medical supply covered by this claim.
NDC-UNIT-OF-MEASURENDC Unit of Measure871872VarcharA code to indicate the basis by which the quantity of the National Drug Code is expressed.
NDC-QUANTITYNDC Quantity873883NumThis field is to capture the actual quantity of the National Drug Code being prescribed on the claim.
HCPCS-RATEHCPCS Rate884897VarcharThis data element is expected to capture data from the HIPAA 837I claim loop 2400 SV206 or UB-04 FL 44.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date898905DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
SELF-DIRECTION-TYPESelf-Direction Type906908NumThis data element is not applicable to this file type.
PRE-AUTHORIZATION-NUMPreauthorization Number909926VarcharA number, code or other value that indicates the services provided on this claim have been authorized by the payee or other service organization, or that a referral for services has been approved. (Also referred to as a Prior Authorization or Referral Number).
FillervarcharSpace Filler for File Format
IHS-SERVICE-INDIHS Service Indicator927927VarcharTo indicate Services received by Medicaid-eligible individuals who are American Indian or Alaska Native (AI/AN) through facilities of the Indian Health Service (IHS), whether operated by IHS or by Tribes.
FILE-HEADER-RECORD-OT
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
DATA-DICTIONARY-VERSIONData Dictionary Version918VarcharA data element to capture the version of the T-MSIS data dictionary that was used to build the file. Use the version number specified on the Cover Sheet of the data dictionary".
SUBMISSION-TRANSACTION-TYPESubmission Transaction Type1919CharA data element to identify the whether the transactions in the file are original submissions of the data, a resubmission of a previously submitted file, or corrections of edit rejects.
FILE-ENCODING-SPECIFICATIONFile Encoding Specification2022CharDenotes which supported file encoding standard was used to create the file.
DATA-MAPPING-DOCUMENT-VERSIONData Mapping Document Version2331CharIdentifies the version of the T-MSIS data mapping document used to build a state submission file. Use the version number specified on the title page of the data mapping document
FILE-NAMEFile Name3239CharA code to identify the subject area to which the records in its file relate. Each T-MSIS submission file should only
contain records for one subject area (i.e., Eligible, Third-party liability, Provider, Managed Care Plan Information,
Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State4041NumA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
DATE-FILE-CREATEDDate File Created4249dateThe date on which the file was created.
START-OF-TIME-PERIODStart of Time Period5057DateThis value must be the first day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span of the data in the file.
END-OF-TIME-PERIODEnd of Time Period5865DateThis value must be the last day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span.
FILE-STATUS-INDICATORFile Status Indicator6666CharA code to indicate whether the records in the file are test or production records.
SSN-INDICATORSSN Indicator6767NumIndicates whether the state uses the eligible person's social security number instead of an MSIS Identification Number as the unique, unchanging eligible person identifier. A state's SSN/Non-SSN designation on the eligibility file should match on the claims and third party liability
files.
TOT-REC-CNTTotal Record Count6878NumA count of all records in the file except for the file header record. This count will be used as a control total to help assure that the file did not become corrupted during transmission.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation83582VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
SEQUENCE-NUMBERSequence Number7982NumTo enable states to sequentially number files, when related, follow-on files are necessary (i.e. update files, replace files). This should begin with 1 for the
original Create submission type and be incremented by one for each Replacement or Update submission for the same reporting period and file type (subject
area).
CLAIM-HEADER-RECORD-OT
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State910VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN2271NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN72121NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID122133NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number134153NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier.

MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

See T-MSIS Guidance Document, "CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers" for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the
same MSIS Identification Number.
https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
CROSSOVER-INDICATORCrossover Indicator154154VarcharAn indicator specifying whether the claim is a crossover claim where a portion is paid by Medicare.
1115A-DEMONSTRATION-IND1115A Demonstration Indicator155155NumIn the claims files this data element indicates whether the claim or encounter was covered under the authority of an 1115A demonstration. In the Eligibility file, this data element indicates whether the individual participates in an 1115A demonstration.
ADJUSTMENT-INDAdjustment Indicator156156VarcharIndicates the type of adjustment record.
ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODEAdjustment Reason Code157159VarcharClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a claim was paid differently than it was billed.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-1Diagnosis Code 1160166VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-1Diagnosis Code Flag 1167167NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-1Diagnosis POA Flag 1168168VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-2Diagnosis Code 2169175VarcharICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes used as a tool to group and identify diseases, disorders, symptoms, poisonings,
adverse effects of drugs and chemicals, injuries and other reasons for patient encounters. Diagnosis codes should be
passed through to T-MSIS exactly as they were submitted by the provider on their claim (with the exception of removing
the decimal). For example: 210.5 is coded as "2105".
DIAGNOSIS-CODE-FLAG-2Diagnosis Code Flag 2176176NumFlag used to identify if associated Diagnosis Code field is reported with ICD-9 or ICD-10 code. Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n,
where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
DIAGNOSIS-POA-FLAG-2Diagnosis POA Flag 2177177VarcharA code to identify conditions that are present at the time the order for inpatient admission occurs; conditions that develop during an outpatient encounter, including emergency department, observation, or outpatient surgery.
POA indicator is used to identify certain preventable conditions that are: (a) high cost or high volume or both, (b) result in the assignment of a case to a Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)* that has a higher payment when present as a secondary diagnosis, and (c) could reasonably
have been prevented through the application of evidence-based guidelines.
*States that do not use the grouper methodology may use CMS-approved methodology that is prospective in nature.
