Each Diagnosis Code Flag is associated with one, and only one, Diagnosis Code in a given file segment record. For example, Diagnosis Code n is associated with Diagnosis Code Flag n, where n can be any integer greater than or equal to 1.
BEGINNING-DATE-OF-SERVICEBeginning Date of Service178185NumFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ENDING-DATE-OF-SERVICEEnding Date of Service186193DateFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date194201DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
MEDICAID-PAID-DATEMedicaid Paid Date202209DateThe date Medicaid paid this claim or adjustment. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), the date the managed care organization paid the provider for the claim or adjustment.
TYPE-OF-CLAIMType of Claim210210VarcharA code to indicate what type of payment is covered in this claim.
TYPE-OF-BILLType of Bill211214NumA data element corresponding with UB-04 form locator FL4 that classifies the claim as to the type of facility (2nd digit), type of care (3rd digit) and the billing record&#x27;s sequence in the episode of care (4th digit). (Note that the 1st digit is always zero.)
CLAIM-STATUSClaim Status215217VarcharThe health care claim status codes convey the status of an entire claim status codes from the 277 transaction set. Only report the claim status for the final, adjudicated claim.
CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORYClaim Status Category218220VarcharThe general category of the claim status (accepted, rejected, pended, finalized, additional information requested, etc.) from the 277 transaction set which is then further detailed in the companion data element claim status.
SOURCE-LOCATIONSource Location221222NumThe field denotes the claims payment system from which the claim was extracted.
CHECK-NUMCheck Number223237NumThe check or electronic funds transfer number.
CHECK-EFF-DATECheck Effective Date238245DateThe date a check is issued to the payee. In the case of electronic funds transfer, it is the date the transfer is made.
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-1Remittance Advice Remark Code 1246250VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-2Remittance Advice Remark Code 2251255VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-3Remittance Advice Remark Code 3256260varcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-4Remittance Advice Remark Code 4261265VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
TOT-BILLED-AMTTotal Billed Amount266278NumThe total amount billed for this claim at the claim header level as submitted by the provider. For encounter records, when Type of Claim value is [ 3, C, or W ], then value must equal amount the provider billed to the managed care plan. Total Billed Amount is not expected on financial
transactions.
TOT-ALLOWED-AMTTotal Allowed Amount279291NumThe claim header level maximum amount determined by the payer as being 'allowable' under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On FFS claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS. On managed care encounters the Allowed Amount is
determined by the managed care organization.
TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMTTotal Medicaid Paid Amount292304NumThe total amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim header level, which is the sum of the amounts paid by Medicaid or the managed care plan at the detail level for the claim.
TOT-MEDICARE-DEDUCTIBLE-AMTTotal Medicare Deductible Amount305317NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP, on this claim at the claim header level, toward the beneficiary&#x27;s Medicare deductible. If the Medicare deductible amount can be identified separately from Medicare coinsurance payments, code that amount in this field. If the Medicare coinsurance and deductible payments cannot be separated, fill this field with the combined payment amount, code Medicare Combined Indicator a '1' and leave Total Medicare Coinsurance Amount unpopulated.
TOT-MEDICARE-COINS-AMTTotal Medicare Coinsurance Amount318330NumThe total amount paid by the Medicaid/CHIP agency or a managed care plan towards the portion of the Medicare allowed charges that Medicare applied to coinsurance.
TOT-TPL-AMTTotal TPL Amount331343NumThird-party liability refers to the legal obligation of third parties, i.e., certain individuals, entities, or programs, to pay all or part of the expenditures for medical assistance furnished under a state plan. This is the total amount denoted at the claim header level paid by the third party.
TOT-OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTTotal Other Insurance Amount344356NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
OTHER-INSURANCE-INDOther Insurance Indicator357357NumThe field denotes whether the insured party is covered under another insurance plan other than Medicare or Medicaid.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection358360NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
SERVICE-TRACKING-TYPEService Tracking Type361362NumA code to categorize service tracking claims. A "service tracking claim" is used to report lump sum payments that cannot be attributed to a single enrollee.
SERVICE-TRACKING-PAYMENT-AMTService Tracking Payment Amount363375NumOn service tracking claims, the payment amount is the lump sum that cannot be attributed to any one beneficiary paid to the provider.
FIXED-PAYMENT-INDFixed Payment Indicator376376NumThis indicator indicates that the reimbursement amount included on the claim is for a fixed payment. Fixed payments are made by the state to insurers or providers for premiums or eligible coverage, not for a particular service. For example, some states have Primary Care Case Management
programs where the state pays providers a monthly patient management fee of $3.50 for each eligible participant under their care. This fee is considered a fixed payment. It is very important for states to correctly identify fixed payments. Fixed payments do not have a defined "medical record"
associated with the payment; therefore, fixed payments are not subject to medical record request and medical record review.
FUNDING-CODEFunding Code377378CharA code to indicate the source of non-federal share funds.
FUNDING-SOURCE-NONFEDERAL-SHAREFunding Source Non-Federal Share379380NumA code to indicate the type of non-federal share used by the state to finance its expenditure to the provider. In the event of two sources, states are to report the portion which represents the largest proportion not funded by the Federal government.
MEDICARE-COMB-DED-INDMedicare Combined Deductible Indicator381381NumCode indicating that the amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP on this claim toward the recipient's Medicare deductible was combined with their coinsurance amount because the amounts could not be separated.
PROGRAM-TYPEProgram Type382383NumA code to indicate special Medicaid program under which the service was provided.
PLAN-ID-NUMBERPlan ID Number384395VarcharA unique number assigned by the state which represents a distinct comprehensive managed care plan, prepaid health plan, primary care case management program, a program for all-inclusive care for the elderly entity, or other approved plans.
PAYMENT-LEVEL-INDPayment Level Indicator396396NumThe field denotes whether the payment amount was determined at the claim header or line/detail level.
For claims where payment is NOT determined at the individual line level (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 1), the claim lines’ associated allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts are left blank and the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amount is reported at the header level only.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines and when applicable, cost-sharing and/or coordination of benefits were deducted from one or more specific line-level payment/allowed amounts (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 2), the allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts on the associated claim lines should sum to the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts reported on the claim header.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines but then cost sharing or coordination of benefits was deducted from the total paid/allowed amount at the header only (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 3), then the line-level paid amount (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) would be blank and line-level allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and header level total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts must all be populated but the line level allowed amounts are not expected to sum exactly to the header level total allowed.
For example, if a claim for an office visit and a procedure is assigned a separate line-level allowed amount for each line, but then at the header level a copay is deducted from the header-level total allowed and/or total paid amounts, then the sum of line-level allowed amounts may not be equal to the header-level total allowed amounts or correspond directly to the total paid amount. If the state cannot distinguish between the scenarios for value 1 and value 3, then value 1 can be used for all claims with only header-level total allowed/paid amounts.
MEDICARE-REIM-TYPEMedicare Reimbursement Type397398NumA code to indicate the type of Medicare reimbursement.
CLAIM-LINE-COUNTClaim Line Count399402NumThe total number of lines on the claim.
FORCED-CLAIM-INDForced Claim Indicator403403NumIndicates if the claim was processed by forcing it through a manual override process.
HEALTH-CARE-ACQUIRED-CONDITION-INDHealthcare Acquired Condition Indicator404404NumThis code indicates whether the claim has a Health Care Acquired Condition. For additional coding information refer to the following site :
https://www.cms.gov/Medicare/Medicare-Fee-for-Service-Payment/HospitalAcqCond/index.html?redirect=/hospitalacqcond/05_​Coding.asp#TopOfPage
OCCURRENCE-CODE-01Occurrence Code 1405406VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-02Occurrence Code 2407408VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-03Occurrence Code 3409410VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-04Occurrence Code 4411412VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-05Occurrence Code 5413414VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-06Occurrence Code 6415416VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-07Occurrence Code 7417418VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-08Occurrence Code 8419420VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-09Occurrence Code 9421422VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-10Occurrence Code 10423424VarcharA code to describe specific event(s) relating to this billing period covered by the claim. (These are From Locators 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, and 36 - Occurrence Codes on the UB04.) These fields can be used for either occurrences or occurrence spans.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-01Occurrence Code Effective Date 1425432dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-02Occurrence Code Effective Date 2433440dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-03Occurrence Code Effective Date 3441448dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-04Occurrence Code Effective Date 4449456dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-05Occurrence Code Effective Date 5457464dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-06Occurrence Code Effective Date 6465472dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-07Occurrence Code Effective Date 7473480dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-08Occurrence Code Effective Date 8481488dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-09Occurrence Code Effective Date 9489496dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-EFF-DATE-10Occurrence Code Effective Date 10497504dateThe start date of the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span codes.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-01Occurrence Code End Date 1505512dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-02Occurrence Code End Date 2513520dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-03Occurrence Code End Date 3521528dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-04Occurrence Code End Date 4529536dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-05Occurrence Code End Date 5537544dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-06Occurrence Code End Date 6545552dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-07Occurrence Code End Date 7553560dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-08Occurrence Code End Date 8561568dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-09Occurrence Code End Date 9569576dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
OCCURRENCE-CODE-END-DATE-10Occurrence Code End Date 10577584dateThe last date that the corresponding occurrence code or occurrence span code was applicable. If occurrence date span is a single day, value must be equal to the value of the associated Occurrence Code Effective Date.
PATIENT-CONTROL-NUMPatient Control Number585604NumA patient's unique number assigned by the provider agency during claim submission, which identifies the client or the client's episode of service within the provider's system to facilitate retrieval of individual financial and clinical records and posting of payment
ELIGIBLE-LAST-NAMEEligible Last Name605634CharThe last name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it
appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification
Number will be used to associate a claim record with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-FIRST-NAMEEligible First Name635664CharThe first name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification Number will be used to associate a claim record
with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-MIDDLE-INITEligible Middle Initial665665CharIndividual's middle initial; middle initial component of full name (e.g. First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name).
DATE-OF-BIRTHDate of Birth666673dateAn individual's date of birth.
HEALTH-HOME-PROV-INDHealth Home Provider Indicator674674NumIndicates whether the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the Health Home care model to provide services for the beneficiary on the claim. Health home providers provide service for patients with chronic illnesses. States that do not specify an eligible individual's health home provider number, if applicable, should not
report claims that indicate the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the health home model.
WAIVER-TYPEWaiver Type675676NumA code for specifying waiver type under which the eligible individual is covered during the month and receiving services/under which claim is submitted.
WAIVER-IDWaiver ID677696VarcharField specifying the waiver or demonstration which authorized payment for a claim. These IDs must be the approved, full federal waiver ID number assigned during the state submission and CMS approval process. Waiver IDs should actually only be the
"core" part of the waiver IDs, without including suffixes for renewals or amendments.
BILLING-PROV-NUMBilling Provider Number697726VarcharA unique identification number assigned by the state to a provider or capitation plan. This data element should represent the entity billing for the service. For encounter records, if associated Type of Claim value equals 3, C, or W, then value must be the state identifier of the provider or entity
(billing or reporting) to the managed care plan.
BILLING-PROV-NPI-NUMBilling Provider NPI Number727736VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the billing entity responsible for billing a patient for healthcare services. The billing provider can also be servicing, referring, or prescribing provider. Can be admitting provider except for Long Term Care.
BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMYBilling Provider Taxonomy737748VarcharThe taxonomy code for the provider billing for the service.
BILLING-PROV-TYPEBilling Provider Type749750NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
BILLING-PROV-SPECIALTYBilling Provider Specialty751752NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
REFERRING-PROV-NUMReferring Provider Number753782VarcharA unique identification number assigned to a provider which identifies the physician or other provider who referred the patient. For physicians, this must be the individual's ID number, not a
group identification number. If the referring provider number is not available, but the physician's Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) ID is on the State file, then the State should use the DEA
ID for this data element.
REFERRING-PROV-NPI-NUMReferring Provider NPI Number783792VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the provider who recommended the servicing provider to the patient.
MEDICARE-HIC-NUMMedicare HIC Number793804NumThe Medicare HIC Number (HICN) is an identifier formerly used by SSA and CMS to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. For many beneficiaries, their SSN was a major component of their HICN. To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) over the course of 2018 and 2019. HICN continue to be used by Medicare for limited administrative purposes after 2019 but starting in 2020 the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries. HICN consists of two components: SSN &
alpha-suffix or (for Railroad IDs) prefix and ID (not always SSN based)
PLACE-OF-SERVICEPlace of Service805806VarcharA data element corresponding with line 24b on the CMS-1500 that indicates where the services took place. This is a pass-through data element that should not be modified or derived when missing unless otherwise specified.
REMITTANCE-NUMRemittance Number807836VarcharThe Remittance Advice Number is a sequential number that identifies the current Remittance Advice (RA) produced for a provider. The number is incremented by one each time a new RA is generated. The first five (5) positions are Julian date following a YYDDD format. The RA is the detailed
explanation of the reason for the payment amount.
DAILY-RATEDaily Rate837843NumThe amount a policy will pay per day for a covered service. In some cases for OT claims this is referred to as a flat rate.
BORDER-STATE-INDBorder State Indicator844844NumA code to indicate whether an individual received services or equipment across state borders. (The provider location is out of state, but for payment purposes the provider is treated as an in-state provider.)
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Coinsurance Paid Amount845857NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their coinsurance for the covered services on the claim. Do not include coinsurance payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Coinsurance Date Paid858865NumThe date the beneficiary paid the coinsurance amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Paid Amount866878NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment for the covered services on the claim. Do not include copayment payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Copayment Date Paid879886NumThe date the beneficiary paid the copayment amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Paid Amount887899NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their deductible for the covered services on the claim. Do not include deductible payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Deductible Date Paid900907DateThe date the beneficiary paid the deductible amount.
CLAIM-DENIED-INDICATORClaim Denied Indicator908908NumAn indicator to identify a claim that the state refused pay in its entirety.
COPAY-WAIVED-INDCopayment Waived Indicator909909NumNot Applicable
HEALTH-HOME-ENTITY-NAMEHealth Home Entity Name910959VarcharA free-form text field to indicate the health home program that authorized payment for the service on the claim or to identify the health home SPA in which an individual is enrolled. The name entered should be the name that the state uses to uniquely identify the team. A "Health Home Entity" can be a designated provider (e.g., physician, clinic,
behavioral health organization), a health team which links to a designated provider, or a health team (physicians, nurses, behavioral health professionals). Because an identification numbering schema has not been established, the entities' names are being used instead.
THIRD-PARTY-COINSURANCE-AMOUNT-PAIDThird Party Coinsurance Amount Paid960972NumThe amount of money paid by a third party on behalf of the beneficiary towards coinsurance.
THIRD-PARTY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDThird Party Coinsurance Date Paid973980DateThe date the third party paid the coinsurance amount
THIRD-PARTY-COPAYMENT-AMOUNT-PAIDThird Party Copayment Amount Paid981993NumThe amount of money paid by a third party on behalf of the beneficiary towards copayment.
THIRD-PARTY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDThird Party Copayment Date Paid9941001DateThe date the third party paid the copayment amount.
HEALTH-HOME-PROVIDER-NPIHealth Home Provider NPI Number10021011VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the health home provider.
MEDICARE-BENEFICIARY-IDENTIFIERMedicare Beneficiary Identifier10121023VarcharThe Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) is a randomly generated identifier used to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. It replaced the previously-used SSN-based Medicare HIC Number (HICN). To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the MBI
over the course of 2018 and 2019. Starting in 2020, the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries.
UNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-NPIUnder Supervision of Provider NPI10241033Varchar[No longer essential - Both data element and associated requirement(s); preserved for file submission integrity. See Data Dictionary v2.3 for specific definition and coding requirement description(s).]
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation10341533VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
PROV-LOCATION-IDProvider Location ID15341538VarcharA code to uniquely identify the geographic location where the provider's services were performed. The Provider Location Identifier values reported on Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim Header Segments must correspond to an active Provider Location Identifier
value on a Provider Location and Contact Info (PRV.003) segment. If a particular license (e.g., a physician's medical license) or provider identifier (e.g., an individual provider's NPI or SSN) is applicable to all of their servicing locations, value "000" (a string of exactly three zeros) can
be used in the PRV.004 or PRV.005, respectively, to represent all locations, however that location identifier must not be attributed to claims or provider bed type info.
ORDERING-PROV-NUMOrdering Provider Number15391568CharThe Medicaid provider ID of the Ordering Provider is the individual who requested the services or items being reported on this service line. Examples include, but are not limited to, provider ordering diagnostic tests and medical equipment or supplies.
[Ordering provider information is only captured at the line level in the X12 837P format but in v3.0.0 of the T-MSIS file layout it is only captured at the header level. This discrepancy will be addressed in a future version of the T-MSIS OT file layout. Until Ordering provider information has been moved from the T-MSIS claim header to the line, there is no need to report it at the header.]
ORDERING-PROV-NPI-NUMOrdering Provider NPI Number15691578VarcharThe NPI of Ordering Provider represents the individual who requested the service or items being reported on this service line. Example include, but are not limited to, provider ordering diagnostic tests and medical equipment or supplies.

[Ordering provider information is only captured at the line level in the X12 837P format but in v3.0.0 of the T-MSIS file layout it is only captured at the header level. This discrepancy will be addressed in a future version of the T-MSIS OT file layout. Until Ordering provider information has been moved from the T-MSIS claim header to the line, there is no need to report it at the header.]
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Liable Amount15791591NumThe total copayment amount on a claim that the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary copayment liability for covered service on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
copayment.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Coinsurance Liable Amount15921604NumThe total coinsurance amount on a claim that the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary coinsurance liability for covered service on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
copayment.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-LIABLE-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Liable Amount16051617NumThe total deductible amount on a claim the beneficiary is obligated to pay for covered services. This amount is the total Medicaid or contract negotiated beneficiary deductible liability for covered services on the claim. Do not subtract out any payments made toward the
deductible.
COMBINED-BENE-COST-SHARING-PAID-AMOUNTCombined Beneficiary Cost Sharing Paid Amount16181630NumThe combined amounts the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible for the covered services on the claim. Only report this data element when the claim does not differentiate among
copayment, coinsurance, and/or deductible payments made by the beneficiary. Do not include beneficiary cost sharing payments made by a third party/ies on behalf of the beneficiary.
CLAIM-HEADER-RECORD-OT-COT00002
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
TOT-COPAY-AMTNum
NATIONAL-HEALTH-CARE-ENTITY-IDVarchar
REFERRING-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
REFERRING-PROV-TYPEVarchar
REFERRING-PROV-SPECIALTYVarchar
BMINum
DATE-CAPITATED-AMOUNT-REQUESTEDDate
CAPITATED-PAYMENT-AMT-REQUESTEDNum
UNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-NPIVarchar
UNDER-DIRECTION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
UNDER-SUPERVISION-OF-PROV-TAXONOMYVarchar
CLAIM-LINE-RECORD-OT
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State910VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number2241NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier.

MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique "key" value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods.

See T-MSIS Guidance Document, "CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers" for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the
same MSIS Identification Number.
https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN4291NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN92141NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
LINE-NUM-ORIGOriginal Line Number142144NumA unique number to identify the transaction line number that is being reported on the original claim.
LINE-NUM-ADJAdjustment Line Number145147NumA unique number to identify the transaction line number that identifies the line number on the adjustment claim.
LINE-ADJUSTMENT-INDLine Adjustment Indicator148148NumA code to indicate the type of adjustment record claim/encounter represents at claim detail level.
LINE-ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODELine Adjustment Reason Code149151VarcharClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a service line was paid differently than it was billed.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID152163NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
CLAIM-LINE-STATUSClaim Line Status164166VarcharThe claim line status codes from the 277 transaction set identify the status of a specific detail claim line rather than the entire claim. Only report the claim line for the final, adjudicated claim.
BEGINNING-DATE-OF-SERVICEBeginning Date of Service167174NumFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, this would be the date on which the service
covered by this claim began. For capitation premium payments, the date on which the period of coverage related to this payment began. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the first day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
ENDING-DATE-OF-SERVICEEnding Date of Service175182DateFor services received during a single encounter with a provider, the date the service covered by this claim was received. For services involving multiple encounters on different days, or periods of care extending over two or more days, the date on which the service covered by this claim ended. For capitation premium payments, the date on
which the period of coverage related to this payment ends/ended. For financial transactions reported to the OT file, populate with the last day of the time period covered by this financial transaction.
REVENUE-CODERevenue Code183186VarcharA code which identifies a specific accommodation, ancillary service or billing calculation (as defined by UB-04 Billing
Manual). Revenue Code should be passed through to T-MSIS exactly as it was billed by the provider on the provider's
837I or UB-04 claim. It is only required on Inpatient, Long-Term Care and Other Fee for Service claims and managed
care encounters that have a valid Type of Bill value. It's not required on financial transactions or non-institutional claims.
PROCEDURE-CODEProcedure Code187194VarcharA field to capture the CPT or HCPCS code that describes a service or good rendered by the provider to an enrollee on the specified date of service.
PROCEDURE-CODE-DATEProcedure Code Date195202DateThe date upon which a reported medical procedure was performed.
PROCEDURE-CODE-FLAGProcedure Code Flag203204NumA flag that identifies the coding system used for an associated procedure code.
PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-1Procedure Code Modifier 1205206VarcharThe procedure code modifier used with an associated procedure code. For example, some states use modifiers to indicate assistance in surgery or anesthesia services.
IMMUNIZATION-TYPEImmunization Type207208Num[No longer essential - Both data element and associated requirement(s); preserved for file submission integrity. See Data Dictionary v2.3 for specific definition and coding requirement description(s).]
BILLED-AMTBilled Amount209221NumThe amount billed at the claim detail level as submitted by the provider. For encounter records, Type of Claim = 3, C, or W, this field should be populated with the amount that the provider billed the managed care plan.
ALLOWED-AMTAllowed Amount222234NumThe maximum amount displayed at the claim line level as determined by the payer as being "allowable" under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On Fee for Service claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS (or PBM). On managed
care encounters the Allowed Amount is determined by the managed care organization.
BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-PAID-AMOUNTBeneficiary Copayment Paid Amount235247NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment for the covered services on a claim line. Do not include copayment payments made by a third party/ies on behalf of the beneficiary. This is a copayment paid for a service in the corresponding claim line for OT and RX claim files. The Beneficiary Copayment Paid Amount is an optional line level data element reported for OT and RX claim file types, only. If the beneficiary copayment paid amount is not available at the claim line level, report the total copayment paid amount in the header level copayment data element.
TPL-AMTTPL Amount248260NumThird-party liability refers to the legal obligation of third parties, i.e., certain individuals, entities, or programs, to pay all or part of the expenditures for medical assistance furnished under a state plan. This is the total amount denoted at the claim detail level paid by the third party.
MEDICAID-PAID-AMTMedicaid Paid Amount261273NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP agency or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim detail level.
MEDICAID-FFS-EQUIVALENT-AMTMedicaid FFS Equivalent Amount274286NumThe amount that would have been paid had the services been provided on a Fee for Service basis.
MEDICARE-PAID-AMTMedicare Paid Amount287299NumThe amount paid by Medicare on this claim. For claims where Medicare payment is only available at the header level, report the entire payment amount on the T-MSIS claim line with the highest charge or the 1st non-denied line. Zero fill Medicare Paid Amount on all other claim lines.
SERVICE-QUANTITY-ACTUALOT-RX-CLAIM-QUANTITY-ACTUAL300310NumThe quantity of a service or product that is rendered for a specific date of service or billing time span as reported by revenue code or procedure code on the claim line. For use with CLAIMOT and CLAIMRX claims. For CLAIMIP and CLAIMOT claims/encounter records, use the Service Quantity Actual field.
SERVICE-QUANTITY-ALLOWEDOT-RX-CLAIM-QUANTITY-ALLOWED311321NumThe maximum allowable quantity of a service that may be rendered per date of service or per month. For use with CLAIMOT and CLAIMRX claims. For CLAIMIP and CLAIMOT claims/encounter records, use the Revenue center -quantity Allowed field. NOTE: One prescription for 100 250 milligram tablets results in Prescription Quantity allowed=100.This field is only applicable when the service being billed can be quantified in discrete units, e.g., a number of visits or the number of units of a prescription/refill that were filled. For prescriptions/refills, use the Medicaid Drug Rebate definition of a unit, which is the smallest unit by which the drug is normally measured; e.g. tablet, capsule, milliliter, etc. For drugs not identifiable or dispensed by a normal unit, e.g. powder filled vials, use 1 as the number of units. The value in Prescription Quantity allowed must correspond with the value in Unit of measure.
TYPE-OF-SERVICEType of Service322324NumA code to categorize the services provided to a Medicaid or CHIP enrollee.
HCBS-SERVICE-CODEHCBS Service Code325325NumA code to indicate that the service represents a long-term care home and community based service or support for an individual with chronic medical and/or mental conditions. The codes help to delineate between acute care and long-term care provided in the home and community setting (e.g.
1915(c), 1915(i), 1915(j), and 1915(k) services).
HCBS-TAXONOMYHCBS Taxonomy326330NumA code to classify the home and community based services listed on the claim into the HCBS taxonomy.
SERVICING-PROV-NUMServicing Provider Number331360VarcharA unique number to identify the provider who treated the recipient. The Servicing Provider Number should be for the individual doctor who rendered the service. If "Servicing" provider and the "Billing" provider such as a sole-practitioner are the same then use the same number in both fields. The
value is conditional as its usage varies by state.
SERVICING-PROV-NPI-NUMServicing Provider NPI Number361370VarcharThe NPI of the health care professional who delivers or completes a particular medical service or non-surgical procedure. The SERVICING-PROV-NPI-NUM is required when rendering provider is different than the attending provider and state or federal regulatory requirements call for a
"combined claim" (i.e., a claim that includes both facility and professional components). Examples are Medicaid clinic bills or critical access hospital claims.
SERVICING-PROV-TAXONOMYServicing Provider Taxonomy371382VarcharThe taxonomy code for the provider who treated the recipient.
SERVICING-PROV-TYPEServicing Provider Type383384NumA code to describe the type of provider being reported.
SERVICING-PROV-SPECIALTYServicing Provider Specialty385386NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection387389NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
TOOTH-DESIGNATION-SYSTEMTooth Designation System390391CharA code to identify the tooth numbering system being used.
TOOTH-NUMTooth Number392393VarcharThe tooth number serviced based on the tooth numbering system identified in the TOOTH-DESIGNATION-SYSTEM field.
TOOTH-QUAD-CODETooth Quad Code394395NumThe area of the oral cavity is designated by a two-digit code.
TOOTH-SURFACE-CODETooth Surface Code396396CharA code to identify the tooth's surface on which the service was performed.
ORIGINATION-ADDR-LN1Origination Address Line 1397456VarcharThe street address of the origination point from which a patient is transported either from home or Long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims, this is only required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
ORIGINATION-ADDR-LN2Origination Address Line 2457516VarcharThe second line of the street address of the destination point to which a patient is transported either from home or Long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims, this is only required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
ORIGINATION-CITYOrigination City517544CharThe name of the origination city from which a patient is transported either from home or a long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims, this is only required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
ORIGINATION-STATEOrigination State545546CharThe ANSI numeric code of the origination state in which a patient is transported either from home or a long term care facility to a health care provider to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa.
ORIGINATION-ZIP-CODEOrigination ZIP Code547555NumThe zip code of the origination city from which a patient is transported either from home or long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa.
DESTINATION-ADDR-LN1Destination Address Line 1556615VarcharThe street address of the destination point to which a patient is transported either from home or Long term care facility to a health care provider for
healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims only. Required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
DESTINATION-ADDR-LN2Destination Address Line 2616675VarcharThe street address of the destination point to which a patient is transported either from home or Long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims only. Required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
DESTINATION-CITYDestination City676703VarcharThe name of the destination city to which a patient is transported either from home or a long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims only. Required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
DESTINATION-STATEDestination State704705CharThe ANSI state numeric code for the U.S. state, Territory, or the District of Columbia code of the destination state in which a patient is transported either from home or a long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims only. Required if state has captured this information, otherwise
it is conditional.
DESTINATION-ZIP-CODEDestination ZIP Code706714NumThe zip code of the destination city to which a patient is transported either from home or long term care facility to a health care provider for healthcare services or vice versa. For transportation claims only. Required if state has captured this information, otherwise it is conditional.
BENEFIT-TYPEBenefit Type715717NumThe benefit category corresponding to the service reported on the claim or encounter record. Note: The code definitions in the valid value list originate from the Medicaid and CHIP Program Data System (MACPro) benefit type list. See Appendix H: Benefit Types
CMS-64-CATEGORY-FOR-FEDERAL-REIMBURSEMENTCMS 64 Category for Federal Reimbursement718719NumA code to indicate the Federal funding source for the payment.
XIX-MBESCBES-CATEGORY-OF-SERVICEXIX MBESCBES Category of Service720724VarcharA code indicating the category of service for the paid claim. The category of service is the line item from the CMS-64 form that states use to report their expenditures and request federal financial participation.
XXI-MBESCBES-CATEGORY-OF-SERVICEXXI MBESCBES Category of Service725727VarcharA code to indicate the category of service for the paid claim. The category of service is the line item from the CMS-21 form that states use to report their expenditures and request federal financial participation.
OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTOther Insurance Amount728740NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation7411240VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
NATIONAL-DRUG-CODENational Drug Code12411252VarcharA code following the National Drug Code format indicating the drug, device, or medical supply covered by this claim.
PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-3Procedure Code Modifier 312551256VarcharThe procedure code modifier used with an associated procedure code. For example, some states use modifiers to indicate assistance in surgery or anesthesia services.
PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-4Procedure Code Modifier 412571258VarcharThe procedure code modifier used with an associated procedure code. For example, some states use modifiers to indicate assistance in surgery or anesthesia services.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date12591266DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
SELF-DIRECTION-TYPESelf Direction Type12671269NumA data element to identify how the beneficiary self-directed the service, i.e. hiring authority (the beneficiary has decision-making authority to recruit, hire, train and supervise the individuals who furnish his/her services), budget authority (The beneficiary has decision-making authority over how the
Medicaid funds in a budget are spent), or both hiring and budget authority.
PRE-AUTHORIZATION-NUMPreauthorization Number12701287VarcharA number, code or other value that indicates the services provided on this claim have been authorized by the payee or other service organization, or that a referral for services has been approved. (Also referred to as a Prior Authorization or Referral Number).
NDC-UNIT-OF-MEASURENDC Unit of Measure12881289VarcharA code to indicate the basis by which the quantity of the National Drug Code is expressed.
NDC-QUANTITYNDC Quantity12901300NumThis field is to capture the actual quantity of the National Drug Code being prescribed on the claim.
PROCEDURE-CODE-MOD-2Procedure Code Modifier 212531254VarcharThe procedure code modifier used with an associated procedure code. For example, some states use modifiers to indicate assistance in surgery or anesthesia services.
IHS-SERVICE-INDIHS Service Indicator13011301NumTo indicate Services received by Medicaid-eligible individuals who are American Indian or Alaska Native (AI/AN) through facilities of the Indian Health Service (IHS), whether operated by IHS or by Tribes.
CLAIM-LINE-RECORD-OT-COT00003
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
HCPCS-RATEVarchar
FILE-HEADER-RECORD-RX
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
DATA-DICTIONARY-VERSIONData Dictionary Version918VarcharA data element to capture the version of the T-MSIS data dictionary that was used to build the file. Use the version number specified on the Cover Sheet of the data dictionary".
SUBMISSION-TRANSACTION-TYPESubmission Transaction Type1919CharA data element to identify the whether the transactions in the file are original submissions of the data, a resubmission of a previously submitted file, or corrections of edit rejects.
FILE-ENCODING-SPECIFICATIONFile Encoding Specification2022CharDenotes which supported file encoding standard was used to create the file.
DATA-MAPPING-DOCUMENT-VERSIONData Mapping Document Version2331CharIdentifies the version of the T-MSIS data mapping document used to build a state submission file. Use the version number specified on the title page of the data mapping document
FILE-NAMEFile Name3239CharA code to identify the subject area to which the records in its file relate. Each T-MSIS submission file should only
contain records for one subject area (i.e., Eligible, Third-party liability, Provider, Managed Care Plan Information,
Inpatient, Long-Term Care, Other, and Pharmacy Claim).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State4041VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
DATE-FILE-CREATEDDate File Created4249dateThe date on which the file was created.
START-OF-TIME-PERIODStart of Time Period5057DateThis value must be the first day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span of the data in the file.
END-OF-TIME-PERIODEnd of Time Period5865DateThis value must be the last day of the reporting month, regardless of the actual date span.
FILE-STATUS-INDICATORFile Status Indicator6666CharA code to indicate whether the records in the file are test or production records.
SSN-INDICATORSSN Indicator6767NumIndicates whether the state uses the eligible person's social security number instead of an MSIS Identification Number as the unique, unchanging eligible person identifier. A state's SSN/Non-SSN designation on the eligibility file should match on the claims and third party liability
files.
TOT-REC-CNTTotal Record Count6878NumA count of all records in the file except for the file header record. This count will be used as a control total to help assure that the file did not become corrupted during transmission.
STATE-NOTATIONState Notation83582VarcharA free text field for the submitting state to enter whatever information it chooses.
FillerVarcharSpace Filler for File Format
SEQUENCE-NUMBERSequence Number7982NumTo enable states to sequentially number files, when related, follow-on files are necessary (i.e. update files, replace files). This should begin with 1 for the
original Create submission type and be incremented by one for each Replacement or Update submission for the same reporting period and file type (subject
area).
CLAIM-HEADER-RECORD-RX
Column NameBusiness NameStartEndData TypeDescription
RECORD-IDRecord ID18VarcharThe Record ID represents the type of segment being reported. The Record ID communicates how the contents of a
given row of data should be interpreted depending on which segment type the Record ID represents. Each type of
segment collects different data elements so each segment type has a distinct layout. The first 3 characters identify
the relevant file (e.g., ELG, PRV, CIP, etc.). The last 5 digits are the segment identifier padded with leading zeros (e.g.,
00001, 00002, 00003, etc.).
SUBMITTING-STATESubmitting State910VarcharA code that uniquely identifies the U.S. State or Territory from which T-MSIS system data resources were received.
RECORD-NUMBERRecord Number1121NumA sequential number assigned by the submitter to identify each record segment row in the submission file. The Record Number, in conjunction with the Record Identifier, uniquely identifies a single record within the submission file.
ICN-ORIGOriginal ICN2271NumA unique number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies an original or adjustment claim.
ICN-ADJAdjustment ICN72121NumA unique claim number assigned by the state's payment system that identifies the adjustment claim for an original transaction.
SUBMITTER-IDSubmitter ID122133NumThe Submitter Identification number is the value that identifies the provider/trading partner/clearing house organization to the state's claim adjudication system.
MSIS-IDENTIFICATION-NUMMSIS Identification Number134153NumA state-assigned unique identification number used to identify a Medicaid/CHIP enrolled individual (except on service tracking payments). Value may be an SSN, temporary SSN or State-assigned eligible individual identifier. MSIS Identification Numbers are a unique 'key' value used to maintain referential integrity of data distributed over multiples files, segments and reporting periods. See T-MSIS Guidance Document, 'CMS Guidance: Reporting Shared MSIS Identification Numbers' for information on reporting the MSIS Identification Numbers ID for pregnant women, unborn children, mothers, and their deemed newborns younger than 1 year of age who share the same MSIS Identification Number. https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/data-and-systems/macbis/tmsis/tmsis-blog/entry/47572
CROSSOVER-INDICATORCrossover Indicator154154VarcharAn indicator specifying whether the claim is a crossover claim where a portion is paid by Medicare.
1115A-DEMONSTRATION-IND1115A Demonstration Indicator155155NumIn the claims files this data element indicates whether the claim or encounter was covered under the authority of an 1115A demonstration. In the Eligibility file, this data element indicates whether the individual participates in an 1115A demonstration.
ADJUSTMENT-INDAdjustment Indicator156156VarcharIndicates the type of adjustment record.
ADJUSTMENT-REASON-CODEAdjustment Reason Code157159VarcharClaim adjustment reason codes communicate why a claim was paid differently than it was billed.
ADJUDICATION-DATEAdjudication Date160167DateThe date on which the payment status of the claim was finally adjudicated by the state. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), use date the encounter was processed by the state.
MEDICAID-PAID-DATEMedicaid Paid Date168175DateThe date Medicaid paid this claim or adjustment. For Encounter Records (Type of Claim = 3, C, W), the date the managed care organization paid the provider for the claim or adjustment.
TYPE-OF-CLAIMType of Claim176176VarcharA code to indicate what type of payment is covered in this claim.
CLAIM-STATUSClaim Status177179VarcharThe health care claim status codes convey the status of an entire claim status codes from the 277 transaction set. Only report the claim status for the final, adjudicated claim.
CLAIM-STATUS-CATEGORYClaim Status Category180182VarcharThe general category of the claim status (accepted, rejected, pended, finalized, additional information requested, etc.) from the 277 transaction set which is then further detailed in the companion data element claim status.
SOURCE-LOCATIONSource Location183184NumThe field denotes the claims payment system from which the claim was extracted.
CHECK-NUMCheck Number185199NumThe check or electronic funds transfer number.
CHECK-EFF-DATECheck Effective Date200207DateThe date a check is issued to the payee. In the case of electronic funds transfer, it is the date the transfer is made.
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-1Remittance Advice Remark Code 1208212VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-2Remittance Advice Remark Code 2213217VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-3Remittance Advice Remark Code 3218222VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
CLAIM-PYMT-REM-CODE-4Remittance Advice Remark Code 4223227VarcharRemittance Advice Remark Codes are used to convey information about remittance processing or to provide a supplemental explanation for an adjustment already described by a Claim Adjustment Reason Code. Each Remittance Advice Remark Code identifies a specific message as
shown in the Remittance Advice Remark Code List. It is a code set used by the health care industry to convey non-financial information critical to understanding the adjudication of a health care claim for payment. It is an external code set whose use is as mandated by the
Administrative Simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountably Act of 1996 (P.L.104-191, commonly referred to as HIPAA).
TOT-BILLED-AMTTotal Billed Amount228240NumThe total amount billed for this claim at the claim header level as submitted by the provider. For encounter records, when Type of Claim value is [ 3, C, or W ], then value must equal amount the provider billed to the managed care plan. Total Billed Amount is not expected on financial
transactions.
TOT-ALLOWED-AMTTotal Allowed Amount241253NumThe claim header level maximum amount determined by the payer as being 'allowable' under the provisions of the contract prior to the determination of actual payment. On FFS claims the Allowed Amount is determined by the state's MMIS. On managed care encounters the Allowed Amount is
determined by the managed care organization.
TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMTTotal Medicaid Paid Amount254266NumThe total amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP or the managed care plan on this claim or adjustment at the claim header level, which is the sum of the amounts paid by Medicaid or the managed care plan at the detail level for the claim.
TOT-MEDICARE-DEDUCTIBLE-AMTTotal Medicare Deductible Amount267279NumThe amount paid by Medicaid/CHIP, on this claim at the claim header level, toward the beneficiary&#x27;s Medicare deductible. If the Medicare deductible amount can be identified separately from Medicare coinsurance payments, code that amount in this field. If the Medicare coinsurance and deductible payments cannot be separated, fill this field with the combined payment amount, code Medicare Combined Indicator a '1' and leave Total Medicare Coinsurance Amount unpopulated.
TOT-MEDICARE-COINS-AMTTotal Medicare Coinsurance Amount280292NumThe total amount paid by the Medicaid/CHIP agency or a managed care plan towards the portion of the Medicare allowed charges that Medicare applied to coinsurance.
TOT-TPL-AMTTotal TPL Amount293305NumThird-party liability refers to the legal obligation of third parties, i.e., certain individuals, entities, or programs, to pay all or part of the expenditures for medical assistance furnished under a state plan. This is the total amount denoted at the claim header level paid by the third party.
TOT-OTHER-INSURANCE-AMTTotal Other Insurance Amount306318NumThe amount paid by insurance other than Medicare or Medicaid on this claim.
OTHER-INSURANCE-INDOther Insurance Indicator319319NumThe field denotes whether the insured party is covered under another insurance plan other than Medicare or Medicaid.
OTHER-TPL-COLLECTIONOther TPL Collection320322NumThis data element indicates that the claim is for a beneficiary for whom other third party resource development and collection activities are in progress, when the liability is not another health insurance plan for which the eligible is a beneficiary.
SERVICE-TRACKING-TYPEService Tracking Type323324NumA code to categorize service tracking claims. A "service tracking claim" is used to report lump sum payments that cannot be attributed to a single enrollee.
SERVICE-TRACKING-PAYMENT-AMTService Tracking Payment Amount325337NumOn service tracking claims, the payment amount is the lump sum that cannot be attributed to any one beneficiary paid to the provider.
FIXED-PAYMENT-INDFixed Payment Indicator338338NumThis indicator indicates that the reimbursement amount included on the claim is for a fixed payment. Fixed payments are made by the state to insurers or providers for premiums or eligible coverage, not for a particular service. For example, some states have Primary Care Case Management
programs where the state pays providers a monthly patient management fee of $3.50 for each eligible participant under their care. This fee is considered a fixed payment. It is very important for states to correctly identify fixed payments. Fixed payments do not have a defined "medical record"
associated with the payment; therefore, fixed payments are not subject to medical record request and medical record review.
FUNDING-CODEFunding Code339340CharA code to indicate the source of non-federal share funds.
FUNDING-SOURCE-NONFEDERAL-SHAREFunding Source Non-Federal Share341342NumA code to indicate the type of non-federal share used by the state to finance its expenditure to the provider. In the event of two sources, states are to report the portion which represents the largest proportion not funded by the Federal government.
PROGRAM-TYPEProgram Type343344NumA code to indicate special Medicaid program under which the service was provided.
PLAN-ID-NUMBERPlan ID Number345356VarcharA unique number assigned by the state which represents a distinct comprehensive managed care plan, prepaid health plan, primary care case management program, a program for all-inclusive care for the elderly entity, or other approved plans.
PAYMENT-LEVEL-INDPayment Level Indicator357357NumThe field denotes whether the payment amount was determined at the claim header or line/detail level.
For claims where payment is NOT determined at the individual line level (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 1), the claim lines’ associated allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts are left blank and the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amount is reported at the header level only.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines and when applicable, cost-sharing and/or coordination of benefits were deducted from one or more specific line-level payment/allowed amounts (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 2), the allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and paid (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts on the associated claim lines should sum to the total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts reported on the claim header.
For claims where payment/allowed amount is determined at the individual lines but then cost sharing or coordination of benefits was deducted from the total paid/allowed amount at the header only (PAYMENT-LEVEL-IND = 3), then the line-level paid amount (MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) would be blank and line-level allowed (ALLOWED-AMT) and header level total allowed (TOT-ALLOWED-AMT) and total paid (TOT-MEDICAID-PAID-AMT) amounts must all be populated but the line level allowed amounts are not expected to sum exactly to the header level total allowed.
For example, if a claim for an office visit and a procedure is assigned a separate line-level allowed amount for each line, but then at the header level a copay is deducted from the header-level total allowed and/or total paid amounts, then the sum of line-level allowed amounts may not be equal to the header-level total allowed amounts or correspond directly to the total paid amount. If the state cannot distinguish between the scenarios for value 1 and value 3, then value 1 can be used for all claims with only header-level total allowed/paid amounts.
MEDICARE-REIM-TYPEMedicare Reimbursement Type358359NumA code to indicate the type of Medicare reimbursement.
CLAIM-LINE-COUNTClaim Line Count360363NumThe total number of lines on the claim.
FORCED-CLAIM-INDForced Claim Indicator364364NumIndicates if the claim was processed by forcing it through a manual override process.
PATIENT-CONTROL-NUMPatient Control Number365384NumA patient's unique number assigned by the provider agency during claim submission, which identifies the client or the client's episode of service within the provider's system to facilitate retrieval of individual financial and clinical records and posting of payment
ELIGIBLE-LAST-NAMEEligible Last Name385414CharThe last name of the individual to whom the services were provided. (The patients name should be captured as it
appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification
Number will be used to associate a claim record with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-FIRST-NAMEEligible First Name415444CharThe first name of the individual to whom the services were provided.(The patients name should be captured as it appears on the claim record, it does not need to be the same as it appears on the eligibility file. The MSIS Identification Number will be used to associate a claim record
with the appropriate eligibility data.)
ELIGIBLE-MIDDLE-INITEligible Middle Initial445445CharIndividual's middle initial; middle initial component of full name (e.g. First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name).
DATE-OF-BIRTHDate of Birth446453dateAn individual's date of birth.
HEALTH-HOME-PROV-INDHealth Home Provider Indicator454454NumIndicates whether the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the Health Home care model to provide services for the beneficiary on the claim. Health home providers provide service for patients with chronic illnesses. States that do not specify an eligible individual's health home provider number, if applicable, should not
report claims that indicate the claim is submitted by a provider or provider group enrolled in the health home model.
WAIVER-TYPEWaiver Type455456NumA code for specifying waiver type under which the eligible individual is covered during the month and receiving services/under which claim is submitted.
WAIVER-IDWaiver ID457476VarcharField specifying the waiver or demonstration which authorized payment for a claim. These IDs must be the approved, full federal waiver ID number assigned during the state submission and CMS approval process. Waiver IDs should actually only be the
"core" part of the waiver IDs, without including suffixes for renewals or amendments.
BILLING-PROV-NUMBilling Provider Number477506VarcharA unique identification number assigned by the state to a provider or capitation plan. This data element should represent the entity billing for the service. For encounter records, if associated Type of Claim value equals 3, C, or W, then value must be the state identifier of the provider or entity
(billing or reporting) to the managed care plan.
BILLING-PROV-NPI-NUMBilling Provider NPI Number507516VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the billing provider responsible for billing for the service on the claim. The billing provider can also be servicing, referring, or prescribing provider; can be admitting provider except for Long Term Care.
BILLING-PROV-TAXONOMYBilling Provider Taxonomy517528VarcharThe taxonomy code for the provider billing for the service.
BILLING-PROV-SPECIALTYBilling Provider Specialty529530NumThis code describes the area of specialty for the provider being reported.
PRESCRIBING-PROV-NUMPrescribing Provider Number531560NumA unique identification number assigned by the state to the provider who prescribed the drug, device, or supply. This must be the individual's ID number, not a group identification number. If the prescribing physician provider ID is not available, but the physician's Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA)
ID is on the state file, then the State should use the DEA ID for this data element
Filler14091450VarcharSpace Filler for File Format
PRESCRIBING-PROV-NPI-NUMPrescribing Provider NPI Number561570VarcharThe National Provider ID (NPI) of the provider who prescribed a medication to a patient.
MEDICARE-HIC-NUMMedicare HIC Number571582NumThe Medicare HIC Number (HICN) is an identifier formerly used by SSA and CMS to identify all Medicare beneficiaries. For many beneficiaries, their SSN was a major component of their HICN. To prevent identify theft, among other reasons, HICN gradually were retired and replaced by the
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) over the course of 2018 and 2019. HICN continue to be used by Medicare for limited administrative purposes after 2019 but starting in 2020 the MBI became the primary identifier for Medicare beneficiaries. HICN consists of two components: SSN &
alpha-suffix or (for Railroad IDs) prefix and ID (not always SSN based)
REMITTANCE-NUMRemittance Number583612VarcharThe Remittance Advice Number is a sequential number that identifies the current Remittance Advice (RA) produced for a provider. The number is incremented by one each time a new RA is generated. The first five (5) positions are Julian date following a YYDDD format. The RA is the detailed
explanation of the reason for the payment amount.
BORDER-STATE-INDBorder State Indicator613613NumA code to indicate whether an individual received services or equipment across state borders. (The provider location is out of state, but for payment purposes the provider is treated as an in-state provider.)
DATE-PRESCRIBEDDate Prescribed614621dateThe date the drug, device, or supply was prescribed by the physician or other practitioner. This should not be confused with the Prescription Fill Date, which represents the date the prescription was actually filled by the provider.
PRESCRIPTION-FILL-DATEPrescription Fill Date622629DateDate the drug, device, or supply was dispensed by the provider.
COMPOUND-DRUG-INDCompound Drug Indicator630630NumIndicator to specify if the drug is compound or not.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Coinsurance Paid Amount631643NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their coinsurance for the covered services on the claim. Do not include coinsurance payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-COINSURANCE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Coinsurance Date Paid665672NumThe date the beneficiary paid the coinsurance amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Copayment Paid Amount644656NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their copayment for the covered services on the claim. Do not include copayment payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-COPAYMENT-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Copayment Date Paid657664NumThe date the beneficiary paid the copayment amount.
TOT-BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-PAID-AMOUNTTotal Beneficiary Deductible Paid Amount673685NumThe amount the beneficiary or his or her representative (e.g., their guardian) paid towards their deductible for the covered services on the claim. Do not include deductible payments made by a third party/s on behalf of the beneficiary.
BENEFICIARY-DEDUCTIBLE-DATE-PAIDBeneficiary Deductible Date Paid686693DateThe date the beneficiary paid the deductible amount.
CLAIM-DENIED-INDICATORClaim Denied Indicator694694NumAn indicator to identify a claim that the state refused pay in its entirety.
COPAY-WAIVED-INDCopayment Waived Indicator695695NumAn indicator signifying that the copay was discounted or waived by the provider (e.g., physician or hospital). Do not use to indicate administrative-level, Medicaid State Agency or Medicaid MCO copayment waived decisions.
